Vega's escape hatch is the Extensibility API for custom transforms (write JS, register a Transform); ECharts' escape hatch is custom series renderItem(params, api) using api.coord()/api.size()/api.style() to draw per-datum graphic elements; ggplot2's is custom Stat/Geom/Coord ggproto objects; Observable Plot and Vega-Lite escape by dropping to D3/Vega; D3 is itself raw JS. Source: https://vega.github.io/vega/docs/transforms/, https://echarts.apache.org/handbook/en/how-to/custom-series/

d3-hierarchy provides treemap, pack, partition, tree, cluster, stratify, hierarchy; d3-force provides forceSimulation + forceCenter/Collide/Link/ManyBody/X/Y/Radial; d3-geo provides geoPath + projections (geoMercator/AlbersUsa/Equirectangular/Orthographic/Stereographic); d3-contour provides contours (marching squares) + contourDensity (2D KDE); d3-hexbin and d3-sankey are PLUGINS not core. Source: https://d3js.org/api

ggplot2 has ~45 geoms (point, line, path, step, bar, col, tile, raster, rect, area, ribbon, boxplot, violin, histogram, freqpoly, bin_2d, hex, density, density_2d, contour, contour_filled, smooth, segment, curve, errorbar, linerange, pointrange, crossbar, text, label, sf, map, polygon, spoke, rug, jitter, count, dotplot, qq, quantile, function, abline/hline/vline). Coords are FIXED: cartesian, fixed, flip, polar, radial, map, quickmap, trans, sf — no arbitrary projection without a coord_*. Source: https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/index.html

Observable Plot marks: Area, Arrow, Bar/barX/barY, Box, Cell, Dot, Line, Link, Rect, Rule, Text, Tick, Vector, Waffle, Geo, Image, Tree, plus statistical marks Bollinger, Contour, Delaunay, Density, Difference, Hexgrid, Linear-regression, Raster. Transforms: Bin, Centroid, Dodge, Filter, Group, Hexbin, Interval, Map, Normalize, Select, Shift, Sort, Stack, Tree, Window. Plot has 'no chart types, only layered marks.' Source: https://observablehq.com/plot/features/marks

NO declarative grammar (Vega, Vega-Lite, ggplot2, D3, Plot, ECharts) provides FFT/STFT/spectral/wavelet transforms as primitives. Spectrogram/audio/ECG-frequency views must precompute DSP upstream or use a custom escape hatch. This is a confirmed universal gap. (Synthesis from full transform-list review across all systems.)

True 3D scientific visualization (volume rendering, isosurface via marching cubes, 3D streamlines/streamtubes, cutting planes) is owned exclusively by VTK and Plotly-3D (scatter3d/surface/mesh3d/isosurface/volume/cone/streamtube); NO 2D declarative grammar covers it. VTK is an imperative source-filter-mapper-actor pipeline, not a grammar. Source: https://book.vtk.org/en/latest/VTKBook/12Chapter12.html, search results on Plotly 3D traces

Reactive Vega (Satyanarayan et al., InfoVis) is the closest prior art to DynaCharting's per-frame streaming: a reactive dataflow DAG of signals driven by event-streams, propagating incremental updates through the transform graph — but it runs on CPU/JS. Running the transform DAG as WebGPU compute passes per frame is NOT done by any prior-art system. Source: https://blog.acolyer.org/2015/12/11/reactive-vega/, https://vega.github.io/vega/docs/signals/

Vega's native transform list ALREADY includes treemap, pack, partition, tree, contour, isocontour, kde2d, force, voronoi, pie, stack, geopath — i.e. most layout/spatial algorithms DynaCharting precomputed upstream for its showcase (treemap, sankey, density, contour). This is positive evidence that ~18 of the 22 showcase views are expressible in a Vega-class declarative basis. Sankey specifically is NOT in core Vega — it is a d3-sankey plugin, signaling it is genuinely hard.

force-directed layout is the canonical frame-coupled / stateful transform: Vega exposes it as a special `force` transform precisely because iterative N-body physics does not fit the pure-functional transform model. Animation in grammars is bolted on (Reactive Vega signals; research-grade 'Animated Vega-Lite' from MIT adds time as an encoding channel). Source: https://vis.csail.mit.edu/pubs/animated-vega-lite/
openQuestions: What is the exact per-frame compute budget DynaCharting targets, and which of the hard layout transforms (treemap squarified, sankey iteration, force N-body, voronoi) can actually meet it as WebGPU compute vs. being too iterative/serial to parallelize well?

Should the escape hatch be raw custom-WGSL-from-JSON (maximum power, security/sandboxing risk, AI-authorability risk) or a higher-level restricted compute-DSL (Vega-expression-like, safe, but re-introduces the loop/control-flow limitation that forces a second escape hatch)?

Does DynaCharting intend to cover 3D at all? If yes, the entire 2D-grammar prior art stops being a guide and the relevant prior art becomes VTK/regl/three.js imperative pipelines — a fundamentally different (non-declarative) model.

How is auto-domaining handled under streaming: does the scale domain re-fit every frame (causing axis jitter) or use a hysteresis/nice-domain policy? Prior-art grammars are mostly batch and dodge this; it is a genuinely new design problem for per-frame streaming.

Spectral transforms (FFT/STFT) are absent from every grammar — should they be added as opaque first-class transform primitives (extending beyond the proven basis, with its own correctness burden) or always pushed to the escape hatch? This decision determines whether spectrogram/audio/ECG are 'native' or 'custom' views.

How many distinct custom-WGSL escape-hatch programs would the 22 showcase views actually need, and does that number undermine the 'one-size-fits-all from a fixed primitive set' claim (i.e., is the escape hatch the exception or the rule for the walled tail)?

viewsDecomp.summary: Decomposed all 23 showcase views (the dir count; the prompt said 22 — candle-overlays is the extra multi-series variant) from raw `{t,streamKey,field,value}` feed data back to the transform/scale/mark primitives that would render them with zero upstream precompute. The required primitive basis is SMALL: ~9 transforms (bin, aggregate/groupBy, formula, window, sort/quantize-walk, stack, KDE-smooth, histogram, treemap-layout), ~4 scales (linear, color/diverging/sequential, band/ordinal, the polar/radial coordinate map), and 7 marks (point, rect, rule/line, area, arc/wedge, ribbon, text — plus the texturedQuad raster fallback). 13 views are native or composed (expressible with linear/aggregate/bin/window/stack + point/rect/line/area marks). 5 force composed-transforms with novel layout/coordinate primitives (treemap squarified pack, sankey ribbon routing, radial polar projection, streamgraph wiggle-stack, ridgeline KDE-stack). 4 force the escape hatch today (contour marching-squares, density KDE field, spectrogram STFT, correlation per-cell matrix) — all are per-pixel/per-cell field computations that the affine+per-draw-item-uniform-color engine genuinely cannot express; they ship as rasterized textures. 1 view (footprint 2D-grid-with-per-cell-magnitude) is the cleanest "not-expressible" case under the CURRENT engine and is the single most important finding: a single global affine + single-float fixed write cannot independently position AND size a 2D cell, which is exactly what the proposed per-instance-color + per-instance-offset (packed instance format) would buy.
viewsDecomp.wall:
The hard wall splits into two distinct kinds, and conflating them is the trap.

KIND 1 — MARK gap (per-cell/per-instance color, NO new compute). weather-radar, correlation-heatmap, and the color half of contour/spectrogram are walled ONLY because the engine gives one uniform color per draw item, so any grid-of-independently-colored-cells must be rasterized to an RGBA8 texture and blitted via texturedQuad (the ENC-532 escape hatch). The transforms behind them range from trivial (weather-radar: upsample+colormap) to real (correlation: rolling Pearson). A single new primitive — a per-instance-color rect-grid mark fed by a sequential/diverging color scale — would collapse weather-radar and correlation-heatmap to native/composed outright. This is the cheapest, highest-leverage gap.

KIND 2 — per-pixel/spectral COMPUTE (genuine escape hatch). density-heatmap (2D Gaussian KDE), spectrogram (STFT/FFT), and the iso-extraction half of contour (marching-squares) require either per-pixel field accumulation or heavyweight signal transforms that have NO expression in affine + filter/bin/aggregate/stack/window. These are the views that justify a custom-WGSL-compute-from-JSON escape hatch. KDE and contour have native-ish approximations (2D-histogram+separable-blur; isolines-as-line2d via a marching-squares CPU transform), but EXACT fidelity = custom shader. FFT has none short of a real spectral primitive.

THE SHARPEST WALL — footprint (not-expressible, and it indicts the CURRENT model, not just the showcase). A true 2D footprint = a price×time grid where each cell has an INDEPENDENT magnitude AND an independent position AND an independent color. The explainer proves rigorously that the current engine cannot do it: with a single global affine transform, a value-driven rect corner always lands at clip(sx·V + tx) — the same clip position for every cell — so you can size a cell OR position it, never both, from one global transform + one float write. It ships downgraded to 80 stacked 1D bars with placement sacrificed and per-cell text/heat dropped. This is the single most important finding because the FIX is exactly the hypothesized layer: PER-INSTANCE offset + PER-INSTANCE color channels (a packed multi-field instance format) bound through band×band scales, plus a text mark for the numerals. Footprint is the proof that the missing primitive is the encoding/channel binding over per-instance attributes — not more compute.

streaming.summary: DynaCharting today has NO data→visual compute stage that runs per-frame from raw numbers: every transform in-tree (AutoScale, CandleAggregator/AggregationManager, DerivedBuffer) is CPU-side and does a FULL O(N) recompute on every touched-buffer event, and there are zero WebGPU compute pipelines (grep for ComputePipeline/dispatchWorkgroups/*.wgsl returns nothing). So the question of "what can run incrementally on a stream" is currently answered by the engine as "nothing is incremental; everything is full-recompute on append." For the hypothesized grammar-of-graphics layer this is the crux scheduling decision. The transform families split cleanly into four streaming classes: (1) trivially incremental — running min/max for scales, running sum/count/mean aggregates, append-only marks, bin/histogram increment, EMA/cumulative windows — all O(1)/O(Δ) per record and the bulk of real-time finance/telemetry views; (2) windowed/bounded-recompute — rolling stats, recent-window FFT/scrolling spectrogram, sliding-window quantiles/KDE — O(W) per hop, bounded by window not by total stream; (3) global-recompute-on-update — sort/rank, treemap/force/sankey layout, global KDE/density, marching-squares contour — O(N) or worse every update, the costly tail; (4) ill-defined under streaming — anything needing a fixed total (percent-of-whole, global normalization, fixed quantile domains) which must be re-baselined as the denominator drifts. The decisive findings: the literature gives genuine incremental algorithms for the class-3 "walls" (resquarify treemap = resize-only stable layout, FM³/interrupt-and-insert online force layout, I-KDE/FlexSketch online density), but they trade exactness/stability for incrementality; and the right engine model is a dirty-flag + scheduler that runs class-1 every frame, class-2 on hop boundaries, and class-3 on a throttled/debounced cadence (decoupled from the data tick) with GPU compute (parallel reduction + decoupled-lookback prefix-sum) as the escape hatch that turns several O(N) globals into ~per-frame-affordable passes.
streaming.markdown:
## Streaming / Incremental Execution — findings for the DynaCharting data→visual layer

### 1. What the engine does today (baseline = full recompute, CPU, no GPU compute)

The repo already has a partial "data-aware" layer, but it is **not incremental and not GPU-resident**. Every transform recomputes globally over the whole buffer on each touched event:

- **AutoScale** (`core/src/viewport/AutoScale.cpp`) — `computeYRange` linear-scans **all** records of every bound buffer each call (O(N)), filtering by visible X. This is the de-facto "scale" primitive and it is full-rescan, not a running min/max.
- **LiveIngestLoop** (`core/src/data/LiveIngestLoop.cpp`) — on every drained batch, re-derives vertex counts and, for auto-scale-Y, loops over **all** candles in the buffer (`for i in 0..numCandles`) recomputing min/max each tick (O(N) per frame).
- **CandleAggregator / AggregationManager** (`core/src/data/CandleAggregator.cpp`, `AggregationManager.cpp`) — `onRawDataChanged` calls `recomputeAggBuffer` which calls `aggregateCandles` over the **entire** raw buffer and `setBufferData` (full replace), not a tail-append. Viewport-tier changes also full-recompute.
- **DerivedBuffer** (`core/src/data/DerivedBuffer.cpp`) — `rebuildOne` re-materializes the whole filtered/ranged output buffer when its source is touched.
- **ChartSession** (`core/src/session/ChartSession.cpp`) — already has the *shape* of a scheduler: a touched-buffer set, recipe `computeCallback`s, `setRecomputeOnViewportChange` / `setRecomputeOnSelectionChange` triggers (D10.3/D26), and an aggregation viewport hook. But the callbacks themselves are full-recompute and there is no debounce/throttle and no frame-budget governor.
- **No GPU compute at all**: `grep -rniE "ComputePipeline|dispatchWorkgroups|computePass"` over `core/src` and `core/include` → 0 hits; no `*.wgsl`. The only GPU transform is the baked affine mat3 (`recomputeMat3` in `core/include/dc/scene/Types.hpp`). So the "execute transforms in WebGPU compute" half of the hypothesis is **greenfield**.
- **Known streaming bug** (memory log): `dc_gpu` instanced backend caches the GPU buffer per `geometryId` and ignores buffer growth (ENC-558) — the showcase slice worked around it with throttled geometry-rebuild + fresh id. This is exactly the "streaming buffer growth" hazard the new model must solve at the backend.

Existing windowing primitives that the incremental model can reuse: per-buffer byte cap + ring eviction (`evictFront`/`keepLast`, Buffer Cache Policy D6.1), `LodManager` (pixels-per-data-unit level switching with hysteresis), `VirtualRange`, `TemporalFilter`.

### 2. Streaming-class taxonomy (the required table)

`N` = total records in buffer; `Δ` = records appended this tick; `W` = window length; `K` = #marks/cells; `F` = FFT size.

| Transform family | Streaming class | Per-update cost (naive) | Mitigation / incremental algorithm |
|---|---|---|---|
| Scale auto-domain (min/max) | 1 trivial | O(N) today → **O(Δ)** | Running min/max accumulator; **monotonic-deque** if windowed so eviction stays O(1) amortized |
| Running aggregate (sum/count/mean) | 1 trivial | **O(Δ)** | Running accumulators; mean = sum/count. Already the natural fit for GMA scalar feed |
| Cumulative / EMA / running stat (Welford var) | 1 trivial | **O(Δ)** | Welford online mean+variance; EMA is O(1)/sample, no window needed |
| Bin / histogram | 1 trivial | **O(Δ)** | Increment target bin(s); only rebin on domain/binwidth change |
| Append-only marks (point/line/candle/OHLC) | 1 trivial | **O(Δ)** | Tail-append to byte buffer + bump vertex count; **but** needs backend dirty/version hook (ENC-558) |
| Candle/time aggregation (OHLC roll-up) | 1 trivial* | O(N) today → **O(Δ)** | Update only the *current* bucket; seal on boundary. Today it's full-recompute — fixable |
| Stack / cumulative-by-group | 1–2 | O(K) | Incremental if append-only categories; reorder/insert forces O(K) restack of that x |
| Rolling stat / moving average / Bollinger | 2 windowed | **O(W)** naive → **O(Δ)** | Ring buffer + running sum (sliding-sum is O(1)/sample); monotonic deque for rolling min/max |
| Sliding-window FFT / scrolling spectrogram | 2 windowed | **O(F log F)** per hop | Hop-triggered, not per-sample; only newest column recomputed, scroll texture. Hop size tunes cost vs smoothness (COLA constraint). Sliding-DFT is O(F)/sample if 1-sample hop needed |
| Rolling quantile / windowed KDE | 2 windowed | O(W log W) | t-digest / order-statistic tree over window; FlexSketch histogram ensemble; I-KDE (update, don't retrain) |
| Sort / rank / top-K | 3 global | **O(N log N)** | Maintain a sorted/heap structure → O(log N) per insert; top-K via bounded min-heap. Avoid full re-sort |
| Treemap (squarified) layout | 3 global | **O(N log N)**, unstable | **resquarify** (D3 `treemapResquarify`): resize-only, positions fixed → stable + cheap on value-only updates; full re-tile only on add/remove |
| Force-directed graph layout | 3 global | iterative, O(N+E)/iter × iters | **Online FM³ / interrupt-and-insert**: keep simulation warm, inject new nodes/edges, run a few refinement iters/frame; SEVis edge-sampling to bound E |
| Sankey / alluvial layout | 3 global | O(nodes+edges) relax | No deployed online variant; throttle + animate (resize-only ribbons, keep node order). Re-solve on topology change only |
| Global density / KDE field | 3 global | O(N·grid) or O(grid·R²) | **I-KDE / xokde++** incremental update; or GPU splat-accumulate per frame; decay old samples for streaming |
| Marching-squares contour | 3 global | O(grid) per field update | Recompute only cells whose scalar field crossed a threshold (dirty-cell set); else full O(grid). GPU-parallel per-cell |
| Percent-of-whole / global normalize | 4 ill-defined | — | Denominator drifts every tick; must pick a *baseline policy* (fixed epoch, decaying total, or accept reflow). Author must declare it in manifest |
| Fixed-domain quantile / z-score vs full history | 4 ill-defined | — | "Whole" is unbounded under streaming; approximate with sketch (t-digest) + explicit reference window |

\*class-1 *if* the manifest declares an append-only/monotonic key (time); otherwise demote.

### 3. Cost of the class-3 globals at streaming rates (the real risk)

At a 30–60 fps render with a multi-Hz data tick, an O(N log N) re-sort or full treemap/force/sankey/contour solve over a growing N **cannot** run on the data thread per tick without dropping frames once N is in the 10⁴–10⁶ range. Concretely:

- A full squarified treemap or marching-squares pass over a 256×256 field is single-to-low-milliseconds on CPU — fine at 1 Hz, **frame-killing** at 60 Hz if N grows or multiple such views coexist.
- Force/sankey are iterative; a "converge from scratch each tick" model is a non-starter — they *must* stay warm between ticks.

This is why the engine's update model cannot be "recompute touched transforms synchronously inside ingest" (which is what `ChartSession`/`AggregationManager` do today).

### 4. Mitigations (ordered by leverage)

1. **Decouple compute cadence from data cadence.** Class-1 → every frame; class-2 → on hop/window-boundary; class-3 → **debounced/throttled** (coalesce N ticks, run at e.g. ≤10 Hz or on idle), running on a worker/async so a stale-but-recent layout shows while the next solves. `ChartSession` already has the trigger hooks; it lacks the throttle + async + frame-budget.
2. **Prefer the incremental algorithm over the exact one** for the walls: resquarify (treemap), warm-start FM³ (force), incremental I-KDE/sketches (density/quantile), dirty-cell marching squares. These are the literature's answer and they trade a small stability/exactness loss for streamability.
3. **Level-of-detail + windowing as cost ceilings.** Reuse `LodManager` (already present) and ring-eviction (D6.1) so class-2/3 operate on a *bounded* W, not the whole history — converts many "global" costs into "windowed."
4. **GPU compute as the escape hatch.** Parallel reduction (running min/max/sum, autoscale domain), decoupled-lookback **prefix-sum/scan** (stacking, cumulative, CDF, bin compaction), per-cell parallel marching-squares, splat-accumulate KDE — all map to WebGPU compute and turn several O(N) globals into one ~per-frame dispatch. This is the same custom-WGSL-from-JSON escape hatch flagged in the showcase frontier (walled tier). Sort is the awkward one (GPU bitonic/radix exists but is heavier than incremental CPU heap maintenance).
5. **Dirty-range, not full-replace.** Replace `setBufferData(full)` patterns (Aggregation/DerivedBuffer) with tail-append + coalesced dirty-range upload (`CpuBufferStore` already supports dirty-range coalescing and `GpuDevice::writeBufferRange`), and fix the per-`geometryId` GPU-buffer-growth cache bug (ENC-558) so append-only marks are truly O(Δ) end-to-end.

### 5. Required engine update/scheduling model (conclusion)

The engine must move from "synchronous full-recompute on touched buffers" to a **tiered, dirty-flag, frame-budgeted scheduler**:

- **Dependency DAG** of dataset → transform → scale → encoding → mark, each node carrying a **streaming-class tag** (1/2/3/4) declared or inferred from the manifest.
- **Three execution lanes:** (a) *per-frame lane* — class-1 running accumulators + append marks + scale domains, cheap, on the render thread; (b) *boundary lane* — class-2 fires on window/hop events; (c) *throttled async lane* — class-3 layouts/density/contour run debounced on a worker or GPU-compute queue, publishing results atomically (double-buffered) so rendering never blocks on them.
- **Incremental-by-default node implementations** (running min/max, sliding sum, resquarify, warm force sim, sketch-based density) with a **fallback to global recompute** only when the manifest declares a non-incremental dependency or the structure changes (topology edit, domain reset).
- **Class-4 transforms require a manifest-declared baseline policy** (fixed-epoch / decaying / explicit reference window); otherwise they are rejected or warned — "percent of whole" has no well-defined streaming semantics without one.
- **Backend dirty/version hook** so GPU buffers track CPU buffer growth (closes ENC-558), making append-only the genuinely O(Δ) common path.

Net: ~70–80% of the showcase's real-time-natural views (candles, OHLC, line, scatter, depth, volume, footprint, ECG, audio) fall in class-1/2 and become truly incremental; the walled tail (treemap, sankey, force, KDE/density, spectrogram, contour) is *streamable* only via class-3 incremental algorithms + throttling + GPU compute, and a few normalization/percent views are class-4 and need an explicit baseline contract.
keyFacts: No GPU compute exists in the engine: grep for ComputePipeline/dispatchWorkgroups/computePass over core/src and core/include returns 0 hits; no *.wgsl files. The only GPU transform is the baked affine mat3 (core/include/dc/scene/Types.hpp recomputeMat3).
AutoScale (core/src/viewport/AutoScale.cpp computeYRange) linear-scans ALL records of every bound buffer each call (O(N)) — it is a full rescan, not a running min/max.
LiveIngestLoop (core/src/data/LiveIngestLoop.cpp) recomputes auto-scale-Y by looping over all candles in the buffer every tick (O(N) per frame).

AggregationManager.recomputeAggBuffer (core/src/data/AggregationManager.cpp) and CandleAggregator.aggregateCandles re-aggregate the ENTIRE raw buffer and setBufferData (full replace) on any touched raw buffer — no tail-append.
DerivedBuffer.rebuildOne (core/src/data/DerivedBuffer.cpp) re-materializes the whole filtered/ranged output buffer when its source is touched.

ChartSession (core/src/session/ChartSession.cpp) already has scheduler scaffolding: a touched-buffer set, recipe computeCallbacks, setRecomputeOnViewportChange/setRecomputeOnSelectionChange triggers — but callbacks are full-recompute with no debounce/throttle/frame-budget.

Existing windowing/LOD primitives to reuse: ring-buffer eviction evictFront/keepLast (Buffer Cache Policy D6.1), LodManager (pixels-per-data-unit level switching with hysteresis), VirtualRange, TemporalFilter; CpuBufferStore supports dirty-range coalescing + GpuDevice::writeBufferRange for incremental tail uploads.

Known streaming bug ENC-558: dc_gpu instanced backend caches the GPU buffer per geometryId and ignores buffer growth; the showcase worked around it with throttled geometry-rebuild + fresh id. Real fix = backend dirty/version hook.

Literature confirms incremental algorithms for the class-3 walls: resquarify treemap (D3 treemapResquarify, resize-only/positions-fixed = stable), online FM3 / interrupt-and-insert force layout, incremental I-KDE and FlexSketch histogram-ensemble for streaming density.

Sliding-window FFT/spectrogram cost is O(F log F) per HOP (not per sample); hop size trades time-resolution/smoothness vs compute; COLA constraint governs valid hop choices. Sliding-DFT is O(F)/sample if 1-sample hop is required.

GPU compute escape hatch maps cleanly: parallel reduction (running min/max/sum, autoscale), decoupled-lookback prefix-sum/scan (stack, cumulative, CDF, bin compaction), per-cell parallel marching-squares, splat-accumulate KDE — turning several O(N) globals into one per-frame WebGPU dispatch.

Class-4 (ill-defined under streaming) = percent-of-whole, global normalization, fixed-domain quantile/z-score-vs-full-history: the denominator/domain drifts every tick, so they require a manifest-declared baseline policy (fixed-epoch / decaying / explicit reference window) or they have no well-defined streaming semantics.
openQuestions: Where should class-3 async compute run in the WASM/browser context — a Web Worker, a WebGPU compute queue, or both — given dc core is single-threaded WASM and the showcase already forwards data on the main thread?

What is the manifest schema for declaring a transform's streaming-class / baseline policy, and should the engine infer the class from transform type + a monotonic-key declaration, or require the AI author to tag it explicitly?

For class-3 incremental layouts (resquarify, warm force), what stability vs exactness budget is acceptable — i.e. does the product tolerate the visual drift these approximations introduce, or does it demand exact periodic re-solves?
Is GPU bitonic/radix sort worth implementing, or is incremental CPU heap/order-statistic maintenance always preferable for the sort/rank/top-K family at expected N?

What concrete frame-budget governor (ms-per-frame ceiling, coalescing window, idle-callback cadence) should the throttled lane use, and how does it interact with the existing ChartSession viewport/selection triggers?
How does double-buffered atomic publish of class-3 results interact with the ENC-558 per-geometryId GPU buffer cache and the scene beginFrame/commitFrame atomicity?

basis.summary: Designed the concrete data-to-visual layer for DynaCharting, anchored to its real engine inventory (10 pipelines, 7 vertex formats, per-DrawItem single-uniform color, baked affine mat3). Minimal complete basis: a TABLE of named typed columns (f32/i32/cat/timestamp) filled tail-append by the existing binary feed; about 11 SCALE types with streaming auto-domain via running reducers; 8 MARKs (point, rule, rect, line, area, arc, text, image) each declaring data-bound channels; and an encoding model markChannel equals scale of a column or expression. Every mark except candle and area accepts only ONE uniform color per draw item, so the single highest-leverage NEW pipeline is a per-instance attributed rect (instancedRectColor) that unlocks per-cell color and size for bars, heatmaps, footprint, and treemap leaves with zero compute. The Candle6 format is the existing proof per-instance multi-channel encoding already works; the design generalizes it. Affine-only transform forces a polar coordinate extension and leaves the iterative, recursive, and spectral tail to a custom-WGSL escape hatch.
basis.markdown:
## DynaCharting Primitive Basis: the data-to-visual layer

Grounded in the engine: 10 pipelines (core/src/pipelines/PipelineCatalog.cpp), 7 vertex formats (core/include/dc/scene/Geometry.hpp), per-DrawItem single uniform color[4] (core/include/dc/scene/Types.hpp:77), baked affine recomputeMat3 scale-plus-translate only (Types.hpp:39-43), binary ingest 13B header (core/src/ingest/IngestProcessor.cpp), WASM surface applyControl/applyDataBatch/setTexturePixels/render/pick (core/wasm/dc_engine_host.cpp:368-387).

Design principle: keep the renderer dumb; add a thin, typed, named-column model plus scales plus encoding that compiles down to the existing vertex formats every frame. Marks emit the exact strides the pipelines demand (validateDrawItem gate, CommandProcessor.cpp:570-576). Only where a mark needs per-instance color or size that no current format carries do we add a pipeline.

### (1) DATASET / TABLE model
A table is a named control-plane resource holding named typed columns. The binary feed fills columns by column id using the existing ingest record format; a column IS a buffer, just typed and named. No new wire format.

| Concept | Definition | Engine mapping |
|---|---|---|
| table | equal-length columns plus row count | new control resource over existing Buffer ids |
| column | name, type, bufferId, dtype | one ingest CpuBuffer (byte vector) reinterpreted by dtype |
| dtypes | f32, i32, cat (dict u32 plus string palette), timestamp (i64 epoch-ms on CPU, no f64 on WebGPU) | f32/i32 native; cat needs host palette; timestamp normalized to f32 offset pre-GPU |
| row append | feed op equals APPEND (op 1) to a column buffer | unchanged; a table's columns append in lockstep |
| updateRange | op equals 2 patches a slice | unchanged |
| derived column | name, expr pure formula | CPU on append (DerivedBuffer precedent core/src/data) v1; GPU formula later |

Named/typed is the missing abstraction: scales need a domain, encodings reference a column by name, auto-domain needs dtype.

### (2) SCALE primitive set
A scale maps a column domain to a visual range, replacing the baked affine for data axes (affine mat3 survives as pan/zoom viewport applied after scales). Auto-domain is a running reducer on append (class-1, O of delta), cheaper than today's full O of N AutoScale computeYRange rescan (core/src/viewport/AutoScale.cpp).

| Scale | Map | Auto-domain | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| linear | d-range to r-range | running min/max | default; subsumes affine sx/tx |
| log | positive to range | running min/max | finance log-price |
| pow/sqrt | x-to-k then linear | running min/max | area-true bubble size equals sqrt |
| time | epoch-ms to range | running min/max i64, f32 offset | f64 trap; base epoch on CPU |
| band | category to slot plus padding | growing ordered set | bar x, heatmap columns |
| point | category to position | growing ordered set | line-per-category, ticks |
| quantile | sample to k buckets | streaming sketch (t-digest/P2) | quantile color bins |
| quantize | uniform cut to k | running min/max plus k | binned color |
| threshold | breakpoints to k | static | choropleth steps |
| sequential color | numeric to ramp | running min/max | heatmap/weather-radar magnitude |
| diverging color | numeric to two-sided ramp | running min/max plus fixed mid | correlation minus1 to plus1 |

Color scales emit RGBA8 per row, the bridge to per-instance color (section 3). Per-pixel color (KDE/spectrogram) uses a 256x1 LUT texture sampled in-shader (setTexturePixels), not per row. Class-4 drifting domains (quantile, diverging-mid, percent-of-whole) require a manifest baseline policy (fixedEpoch, decaying, or referenceWindow).

### (3) MARK set plus channels plus pipeline mapping
8 marks; channel equals visual attribute bound to scale of a column-or-expr or a constant.

| Mark | Channels | Existing pipeline | NEW? |
|---|---|---|---|
| point | x,y,size,color,opacity,shape | points@1 (Pos2_Clip) uniform; instancedRect@1 for size | YES per-point color: instancedPointColor |
| rule | x,y,x2,y2,color,width | line2d@1 / lineAA@1 (Rect4 AA width dash) | per-rule color reuses per-instance color |
| rect | x,y,x2,y2,color,opacity,cornerRadius | instancedRect@1 (Rect4 16B) but ONE uniform color per item (Types.hpp:77) | YES keystone: instancedRectColor (Rect4 plus RGBA8) |
| line | x,y,color-of-series,width | line2d@1 / lineAA@1 | no, one color per series equals one item |
| area | x,y,y2,color,opacity | triGradient@1 (Pos2Color4 per-vertex RGBA) / triSolid@1 | no, Pos2Color4 carries per-vertex color |
| arc | theta,theta2,radius,radius2,color | triSolid@1/triGradient@1 wedge plus polar coord | YES coordinate: affine cannot do polar |
| text | x,y,text,size,color | textSDF@1 (Glyph8 32B), host GlyphAtlas null must be wired | wire atlas (gap not new pipeline) |
| image | x,y,x2,y2,texture | texturedQuad@1 (Pos2Uv4) plus setTexturePixels | no, escape hatch for KDE/spectrogram/contour-fill |

Keystone: every mark except candle and area collapses to ONE uniform color per draw item, which is why the showcase rasterizes weather-radar, correlation, and footprint to textures via texturedQuad. The Candle6 format (x,open,high,low,close,halfWidth per instance plus colorUp/colorDown two-way select, Types.hpp:80-81) is the existing proof the engine can do per-instance multi-channel encoding; it just hard-codes it to candles. Generalizing into instancedRectColor (per-instance x0,y0,x1,y1 plus packed RGBA8 plus optional scalar value) is the single highest-leverage addition: it natively unlocks per-bar-color bars, heatmaps, weather-radar, correlation, treemap leaves, and the price-by-time footprint grid, with zero compute, because color and size are pre-resolved per row by the scale stage. This closes the VIEWS-DECOMP KIND-1 mark gap and the footprint not-expressible case (independent per-cell position AND size AND color).

New additions ranked: (1) instancedRectColor per-instance RGBA8 rect (unblocks about 6 walled views); (2) instancedPointColor per-point color/size scatter; (3) polar coordinate transform (a non-affine projection slot replacing the single baked mat3) for arc/pie/radar/sunburst. Everything else maps to the existing 10.

### (4) ENCODING / channel-binding model
encoding equals a map of channel to a binding of {scale, field-or-expr, value}. A mark instance is emitted per table row; each channel resolves scale of row-field to a number (position/size) or RGBA8 (color), packed into the mark's target vertex format. value equals constant, expr equals derived formula, scale omitted equals identity. Compilation: (table, mark, encoding) produces a DrawItem plus a Geometry whose buffer the encode pass writes each frame (class-1 incremental, only appended rows). The encode pass slots between applyDataBatch/store-sync and render (clean insertion point per GPU-COMPUTE brief).

### JSON examples

Bar (raw rows category, amount):

    {"tables":[{"id":"sales","columns":[{"name":"category","dtype":"cat"},{"name":"amount","dtype":"f32"}]}],
     "scales":[{"id":"x","type":"band","field":"sales.category","range":[0,800],"padding":0.1},
      {"id":"y","type":"linear","field":"sales.amount","domainAuto":true,"range":[600,0]},

### Minimality justification
8 marks is the convergent prior-art irreducible set (Vega 12 reduce to about 9; drops path/shape/trail/group composites), covering all 13 native plus 5 composed showcase views. About 11 scales matches Vega/D3 vocabulary, none redundant. 3 new additions (per-instance-color rect, per-instance-color point, polar coordinate) are the complete delta vs the existing 10 and let the affine-only single-color engine express the native-plus-composed roughly 70 percent of the chart universe without compute. Escape hatch remains required for the tail (iterative/recursive layout, spectral, topology-extraction): texturedQuad plus setTexturePixels covers field-color today; custom-WGSL-compute-from-JSON covers the rest.
keyFacts: Engine confirms ONE uniform color per draw item: DrawItem.color[4] at core/include/dc/scene/Types.hpp:77; every mark except candle and area gets a single RGBA, the root cause the showcase rasterizes heatmaps, correlation, footprint to textures.

Candle6 vertex format (core/include/dc/scene/Geometry.hpp:11, 24B equals x/open/high/low/close/halfWidth) plus DrawItem.colorUp/colorDown (Types.hpp:80-81) is the EXISTING proof the engine already does per-instance multi-channel encoding with two-way color select; proposed instancedRectColor generalizes exactly this.

10 pipelines and 7 vertex formats are the immovable compile target (PipelineCatalog.cpp lines 16-25; Geometry.hpp strideOf 8/16/24/32/12/24/16 bytes); CommandProcessor.cpp:570-576 hard-rejects format mismatch so marks must emit exact strides.

Baked affine recomputeMat3 (Types.hpp:39-43) is scale-plus-translate only (no rotation or shear); serves as post-scale pan/zoom viewport, but arc, pie, radar need a NEW non-affine polar coordinate slot the affine cannot express.

Proposed MARK set equals 8 (point, rule, rect, line, area, arc, text, image); SCALE set about 11 (linear, log, pow, sqrt, time, band, point, quantile, quantize, threshold, sequential/diverging color); TABLE model equals named typed columns f32/i32/cat/timestamp filled by the UNCHANGED 13-byte ingest record (op append/updateRange).

Single highest-leverage NEW pipeline equals instancedRectColor (per-instance Rect4 plus packed RGBA8, about 20-32B per instance): unlocks per-cell color and size for bars, weather-radar, correlation-heatmap, treemap leaves, and the footprint price-by-time grid with ZERO compute, closing the VIEWS-DECOMP KIND-1 mark gap and the footprint not-expressible case.

Streaming auto-domain replaces today's full O of N rescan (AutoScale computeYRange, core/src/viewport/AutoScale.cpp) with per-column running reducers: min/max for linear/log/time, growing ordered set for band/point, streaming sketch for quantile (class-1 O of delta per append).
timestamp columns must stay i64 epoch-ms on CPU and be normalized to an f32 offset before any GPU or scale use; WebGPU has no f64 and f32 mantissa overflows at about 16.7M ms.

WASM host gaps: textSDF GlyphAtlas is null in dc_engine_host ensureRenderer (text mark unusable in browser until wired); embind surface (dc_engine_host.cpp:368-387) exposes only applyControl/applyDataBatch/setTexturePixels/render/pick and needs table/scale/encoding control commands added.

Encoding model: markChannel from a binding of scale, field-or-expr, or value; one mark instance per table row; compilation produces a DrawItem plus Geometry whose buffer an encode pass writes incrementally each frame, slotting between applyDataBatch/store-sync and render.

Escape hatch stays mandatory: texturedQuad@1 plus setTexturePixels (Pos2Uv4) covers per-pixel field color (KDE/spectrogram/contour-fill) today; custom-WGSL-compute-from-JSON required for iterative/recursive-layout/spectral/topology tail.

Color scales emit RGBA8 per row (continuous ramp sampled CPU-side or from a 256x1 LUT texture via setTexturePixels in-shader for per-pixel fields), feeding the per-instance color channel of instancedRectColor and instancedPointColor.
openQuestions: Should the encode/pack pass (column plus scale to vertex bytes) run on CPU (WASM, reusing core/src/data DerivedBuffer precedent, single-threaded today) or as a WebGPU compute pass writing a Storage buffer, and at what N does GPU win given no buffer currently has BufferUsage Storage (DawnDevice.cpp:25 kStreamBufferUsage lacks Storage)?
Exact byte layout for instancedRectColor: RGBA8 packed into a u32 (20B per instance) vs 4 f32 color (32B)? Packed u32 halves bandwidth but needs an unpack in the instance shader.

How does the polar coordinate-projection slot integrate with the single baked mat3 viewport: a per-DrawItem projection-type enum feeding a different vertex shader, or CPU pre-projection of arc geometry into clip space (cheaper but loses GPU-side pan/zoom on radial views)?
cat dtype needs a host-side dictionary/palette that grows under streaming: where does the string-to-u32 palette live and how is it kept in sync with band/point scale domains that auto-grow?

Does adding table/scale/encoding as first-class control resources bloat the frame-transaction snapshot-plus-atomic-swap (Scene.cpp six flat maps), or should the data-model live beside the Scene rather than inside it?

For class-4 drifting domains (quantile, diverging-midpoint, percent-of-whole) the manifest must declare a baseline policy (fixedEpoch/decaying/referenceWindow): what is the default and is it author-overridable per scale?

transforms.summary: DynaCharting can host an in-engine grammar-of-graphics transform layer by inserting a transform stage between data-ingest (applyDataBatch -> setCpuData) and the render walk (renderer_->render) at dc_engine_host.cpp:206-220, the same seam the existing CPU aggregators already prove out. I propose a 4-tier transform catalog: CORE (filter, formula, bin, aggregate, stack, sort, window, sample/lod) running on WASM-CPU for v1 with a GPU-compute fast path for large-N parallel reductions/scans; LAYOUT (treemap, partition, pack, force, sankey, dendrogram, geo-projection) which is sequential/recursive/iterative and stays WASM-CPU except force (GPU N-body) — throttled off the data tick; SPATIAL/FIELD (kde, contour, hexbin, regridding) which is embarrassingly parallel and the prime GPU-compute target; and SPECTRAL (fft/stft) which is the canonical custom escape-hatch case. Transforms form a typed DAG of named-column datasets (column = {name, dtype, length, bytes}), each node consuming and emitting columns, terminating in an encoding that packs columns into the exact fixed vertex format a pipeline requires. The escape hatch is a manifest-declared WGSL compute kernel (or a restricted expression DSL for per-row math) with declared typed input/output column bindings, a validation/sandboxing contract (no dynamic alloc, bounded dispatch, storage-buffer-only I/O, f32-only), bought precisely for the spectral/field/custom tail that {bin,aggregate,window,stack} cannot express. A streaming scheduler runs class-1 every frame, class-2 on window-hop boundaries, class-3 on a throttled cadence.
