# Transform / Compute Layer Design for DynaCharting

## 0. Where this plugs in (the seam)

The engine loop today is, per `core/wasm/dc_engine_host.cpp` (lines 160–220):

```
applyDataBatch(bytes) -> IngestProcessor (fills anonymous byte-buffers)
render(w,h):
    syncAllBuffers()           // store_.setCpuData mirrors ingest buffers
    renderer_->render(scene_, store_, w, h)
```

The transform layer inserts a **TransformGraph evaluation pass** between `syncAllBuffers()` and `renderer_->render(...)` (dc_engine_host.cpp:218–220). This is the exact seam the GPU-COMPUTE brief identifies and the same one the existing CPU transforms (`core/src/data/CandleAggregator.cpp`, `AggregationManager.cpp`, `DerivedBuffer.cpp`) already exploit — they re-aggregate raw byte-buffers and `setBufferData` packed float geometry. That is the negative-space precedent: those are *hardcoded recipes*; this design generalizes them into a *manifest-declared typed DAG*.

Critically, the **terminal node of every transform chain must emit columns that an encoding step packs into one of the 8 fixed vertex formats** (`core/include/dc/scene/Geometry.hpp`): `Pos2_Clip(8B)`, `Rect4(16B)`, `Candle6(24B)`, `Glyph8(32B)`, `Pos2Alpha(12B)`, `Pos2Color4(24B)`, `Pos2Uv4(16B)`. `CommandProcessor::validateDrawItem` (CommandProcessor.cpp:570–576) hard-rejects mismatched formats, so the encoder, not the transform, owns the final byte-packing.

## 1. Dataflow model: typed column DAG

**Dataset** = an ordered set of named **Columns**. A Column is `{ name, dtype, length, bytes }` where `dtype ∈ {f32, i32, u32, u8, f32x2, utf8-offset}` (WebGPU is f32-only on GPU — GPU-COMPUTE brief: no f64; epoch-ms must stay CPU/i32-pair). Length is row count (or bin count, or node count downstream).

A **Transform** is a pure node: `inputs: ColumnRef[] -> outputs: Column[]`. Nodes reference upstream outputs by `id.field`, forming a DAG. Evaluation is topological; a per-node **dirty flag** (driven by the touched-buffer set already in `ChartSession`, STREAMING brief) gates recompute. The DAG terminates in **encode** nodes that bind output columns to mark channels and pack to a vertex format.

```
dataset(raw columns) -> [transform DAG] -> encode(channel<-column) -> geometry(vertex format) -> pipeline
                                            ^ scale(domain->range) applied here, replacing baked affine
```

Typing rules: each transform declares an output schema as a function of input schema (e.g. `bin` emits `{bin0:f32, bin1:f32, count:u32}`; `aggregate` emits group-keys + reduced measures). The graph compiler validates dtype compatibility at manifest-load (fail fast), and that the terminal encode's packed stride equals the pipeline's `requiredVertexFormat` stride.

Chaining is by column reference, **not** by re-serializing to the 13-byte ingest format — intermediate columns live in a transform-side `ColumnStore` (a typed sibling of `CpuBufferStore`); only the final encoded geometry is written via `store_.setCpuData` / `writeBufferRange` for GPU upload (CpuBufferStore dirty-range coalescing reused unchanged).

## 2. Tiered transform catalog

| Transform | Tier | Execution target | Streaming class | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **filter** (predicate keep/drop) | core | WASM-CPU (GPU: stream-compaction scan if N>~100k) | 1 incremental | per-row; GPU needs prefix-sum compaction |
| **formula/calculate** (per-row expr) | core | WASM-CPU; GPU-compute for N>~200k | 1 incremental | expression DSL §5; embarrassingly parallel |
| **bin** (1D/2D histogram bucket) | core | WASM-CPU; **GPU-compute** (atomic-u32 into shared bins) for large-N | 1 incremental (increment on append) | GPU image-histogram pattern; workgroup-shared bins |
| **aggregate/groupby+reduce** (sum/mean/min/max/count/p50) | core | WASM-CPU; **GPU parallel reduction** for large-N | 1 (sum/count/mean/min/max), 2 (quantiles) | quantiles need windowed/full pass |
| **stack** (cumulative offset within group/x) | core | WASM-CPU; **GPU decoupled-lookback prefix-sum** | 1 if append-only, 3 if normalize-to-whole | normalized/streamgraph-wiggle = class-4 baseline policy |
| **sort/rank** | core | WASM-CPU | 3 global-recompute | order changes globally on insert; rarely per-frame |
| **window/rolling** (SMA/EMA/rolling-stat) | core | WASM-CPU (EMA O(1); rolling O(W)) | 1 (EMA/cumulative), 2 (fixed-window) | EMA already in `core/math/Ema.cpp` |
| **sample/lod** (M4/min-max band, decimate) | core | WASM-CPU; **GPU** min-max reduction for 1M+ pts | 2 (viewport-hop) | LodManager hysteresis exists; trigger on viewport change |
| **treemap** (squarified) | layout | WASM-CPU only | 3 (resquarify=resize-stable) | sequential data-dependent recursion; not GPU-friendly |
| **partition/icicle/sunburst** | layout | WASM-CPU | 3 | recursive hierarchy walk |
| **pack** (circle packing) | layout | WASM-CPU | 3 | iterative collision relaxation |
| **force/graph** (N-body layout) | layout | **GPU-compute** (multi-pass per frame), CPU fallback | iterative/stateful (engine-owned sim state) | GraphWaGu/cosmos.gl prove WebGPU @100k nodes; frame-coupled |
| **sankey** (node x-layer + ribbon route) | layout | WASM-CPU | 3 | sequential node placement + ribbon tessellation |
| **dendrogram/tidy-tree** (Reingold-Tilford) | layout | WASM-CPU | 3 | recursive |
| **geo-projection** (mercator/albers/ortho) | layout | WASM-CPU (CPU project) or GPU (vertex-stage func) | 1 per-vertex | needs pre-baked boundary polygons; non-affine, replaces baked mat3 |
| **kde/density** (2D Gaussian splat) | spatial/field | **GPU-compute** (splat-accumulate) | 2 (windowed), 3 (global) | I-KDE for streaming; prime GPU case |
| **contour/marching-squares** | spatial/field | **GPU-compute** (per-cell parallel) -> line2d, or CPU | 3 (full-field recompute) | variable-cardinality output; lines via line2d |
| **hexbin** | spatial/field | WASM-CPU or GPU (atomic bin) | 1 incremental | like bin with hex lattice |
| **interpolation/regridding** | spatial/field | **GPU-compute** | 2 | resample to grid -> texturedQuad or instanced rects |
| **fft/stft** | spectral | **GPU-compute** (Stockham radix-2) or WASM-CPU | 2 (per-hop O(F log F)) | **canonical escape-hatch case**; COLA hop constraint |

**Execution-target rationale (from GPU-COMPUTE brief):**
- **WASM-CPU is v1 for all core + all layout.** Single-threaded WASM (CMakeLists.txt:1853) is fine for class-1 O(Δ) work and for sequential/recursive layout which has *no parallelism to exploit anyway*. This is the lowest-risk path and reuses the existing recipe machinery.
- **GPU-compute is justified only where work is large-N AND embarrassingly parallel:** bin/aggregate reductions, stack scans, KDE splat, contour per-cell, FFT. Adding compute is *additive*: `DawnDevice` already holds a live `wgpu::Device`/`Queue` (DawnDevice.hpp:246) and `waitUntil()` already implements the async readback pump. The prerequisite is `BufferUsage::Storage` (today all buffers are `Vertex|Index|CopyDst|CopySrc`, DawnDevice.cpp:25) — a one-line usage extension plus a `createComputePipeline`/`beginComputePass` path on `GpuDevice`.
- **force is the special case:** GPU-friendly but stateful across frames (frame N depends on N-1), so it needs engine-owned simulation buffers, not a stateless manifest re-eval.

**Streaming scheduler (STREAMING brief):** class-1 every frame; class-2 on window-hop boundaries; class-3 on a throttled/debounced cadence decoupled from the data tick. Manifest declares `recomputePolicy: {everyFrame | onHop(hopSize) | throttled(ms) | onViewportChange}` per node. Class-4 (percent-of-whole, global z-score) requires an explicit `baseline: {fixedEpoch | decaying(halfLife) | window(n)}` declaration or has no well-defined streaming semantics.

## 3. The escape hatch (custom WGSL compute from JSON)

Two flavors, declared in the manifest:

**(a) Expression DSL** (the small per-row/per-bin math) — see §5. Covers filter predicates and formula columns without a full shader.

**(b) Custom WGSL compute kernel** — for the spectral/field/bespoke tail (FFT, custom DSP, novel layout). Manifest node:

```json
{ "id": "stft", "transform": "customCompute",
  "wgsl": "@compute @workgroup_size(256) fn main(...) { ... }",
  "inputs":  [{ "binding": 0, "column": "audio.sample", "dtype": "f32", "access": "read" }],
  "outputs": [{ "binding": 1, "name": "magnitude", "dtype": "f32", "length": "frames*bins", "access": "write" }],
  "uniforms": { "fftSize": 1024, "hop": 256 },
  "dispatch": { "x": "frames", "y": 1, "z": 1 } }
```

**Sandboxing / validation contract:**
- I/O **only** through declared storage-buffer bindings (no global state, no host calls). The compiler binds exactly the named input/output columns; the kernel cannot reach other buffers.
- **No dynamic allocation, no recursion, no unbounded loops** (WGSL forbids these natively, so this is mostly free).
- Static limits enforced at load against WebGPU defaults (GPU-COMPUTE brief): `workgroup_size ≤ 256`, workgroup storage ≤ 16 KB, dispatch dims computed from declared column lengths and capped at 65535/dim, ≤ 8 storage buffers/stage, storage binding ≤ 128 MiB. f32/f16/i32/u32 only — **no f64** (so epoch-ms inputs must be pre-normalized to a relative f32 on the CPU side).
- Output column length must be a **declarable function of input lengths** (so variable-cardinality topology like marching-squares emits to a *max-bounded* buffer with an atomic count, then a compaction pass — the engine owns the cap).
- WGSL is validated by Dawn's own shader validator at pipeline creation; a creation failure rejects the manifest node (no partial render), mirroring `frameFailed` rejection semantics.

**What it buys:** exactly the four walls the CHART-UNIVERSE/VIEWS-DECOMP briefs identify as inexpressible in `{filter,bin,aggregate,stack,window,sort}` — FFT/STFT (no grammar has it), 2D KDE field, marching-squares iso-extraction, and bespoke per-cell field math. It is the same escape-hatch pattern every prior-art system ships (Vega custom transforms, ECharts renderItem, ggplot2 custom Stat) — PRIOR-ART brief — but realized as GPU compute rather than CPU JS.

## 4. Expression sub-language (formula/filter)

A **restricted, side-effect-free expression grammar** (intentionally a strict subset, like Vega's expression language which forbids assignment/loops/`new`):

- **Operands:** column refs (`d.close`), literals, `bin`/`row`/`index` context vars.
- **Operators:** `+ - * / %`, `< <= > >= == !=`, `&& || !`, ternary `?:`.
- **Functions:** `min,max,abs,sqrt,pow,log,log2,exp,floor,ceil,round,clamp,sign,sin,cos,atan2,isNaN`, and reducers in aggregate context (`sum,mean,count,p50`).
- **No** assignment, no loops, no user functions, no host access. Deterministic and vectorizable.
- The SAME AST compiles to **either** a WASM-CPU tight loop **or** a generated WGSL body, so `formula`/`filter` get the GPU fast path for free when N is large.

Example: `filter`: `"expr": "d.volume > 0 && d.close >= d.open"`; `formula`: `"as": "ret", "expr": "log(d.close / d.prevClose)"`.

## 5. Concrete JSON examples

### (a) Histogram (bin + count)

```json
{
  "dataset": { "id": "trades", "columns": [{ "name": "ret", "dtype": "f32" }] },
  "transforms": [
    { "id": "h", "transform": "bin", "input": "trades.ret",
      "params": { "extent": "auto", "maxbins": 40 },
      "output": ["bin0", "bin1"] },
    { "id": "c", "transform": "aggregate", "input": "trades",
      "params": { "groupby": ["h.bin0"], "reduce": [{ "op": "count", "as": "n" }] } }
  ],
  "encode": {
    "mark": "rect", "pipeline": "instancedRect@1",
    "channels": {
      "x":  { "scale": { "type": "linear", "domain": "auto" }, "field": "h.bin0" },
      "x2": { "scale": { "type": "linear", "domain": "auto" }, "field": "h.bin1" },
      "y":  { "scale": { "type": "linear", "domain": "auto" }, "field": "c.n" },
      "y2": { "value": 0 }
    }
  }
}
```
Execution: `bin` + `aggregate(count)` are class-1 (increment on append), WASM-CPU by default; GPU atomic-bin path if N large.

### (b) Streamgraph (stack)

```json
{
  "dataset": { "id": "flow", "columns": [
    { "name": "t", "dtype": "i32" }, { "name": "series", "dtype": "u32" }, { "name": "v", "dtype": "f32" }
  ]},
  "transforms": [
    { "id": "s", "transform": "stack", "input": "flow",
      "params": { "x": "flow.t", "groupby": "flow.series", "value": "flow.v",
                  "offset": "wiggle", "order": "inside-out",
                  "baseline": { "decaying": { "halfLife": 5000 } } },
      "output": ["y0", "y1"] }
  ],
  "encode": {
    "mark": "area", "pipeline": "triGradient@1",
    "channels": {
      "x":  { "scale": { "type": "time" }, "field": "flow.t" },
      "y":  { "scale": { "type": "linear", "domain": "auto" }, "field": "s.y0" },
      "y2": { "scale": { "type": "linear", "domain": "auto" }, "field": "s.y1" },
      "fill": { "scale": { "type": "ordinal", "scheme": "category" }, "field": "flow.series" }
    },
    "recomputePolicy": { "throttled": 100 }
  }
}
```
Execution: `stack` with `offset:wiggle` is class-4 (wiggle baseline drifts) — hence the explicit `baseline` policy. Prefix-sum on GPU (decoupled-lookback) for large series.

### (c) Treemap (layout)

```json
{
  "dataset": { "id": "tree", "columns": [
    { "name": "id", "dtype": "u32" }, { "name": "parent", "dtype": "i32" }, { "name": "value", "dtype": "f32" }
  ]},
  "transforms": [
    { "id": "hier", "transform": "stratify",
      "params": { "id": "tree.id", "parent": "tree.parent" } },
    { "id": "tm", "transform": "treemap", "input": "hier",
      "params": { "value": "tree.value", "tile": "squarify", "padding": 1,
                  "rect": [0, 0, 1, 1] },
      "output": ["x0", "y0", "x1", "y1", "depth"],
      "recomputePolicy": { "throttled": 250 } }
  ],
  "encode": {
    "mark": "rect", "pipeline": "instancedRect@1",
    "channels": {
      "x":  { "scale": { "type": "linear", "domain": [0,1] }, "field": "tm.x0" },
      "y":  { "scale": { "type": "linear", "domain": [0,1] }, "field": "tm.y0" },
      "x2": { "scale": { "type": "linear", "domain": [0,1] }, "field": "tm.x1" },
      "y2": { "scale": { "type": "linear", "domain": [0,1] }, "field": "tm.y1" },
      "fill": { "scale": { "type": "sequential", "scheme": "viridis" }, "field": "tm.depth" }
    }
  }
}
```
Execution: WASM-CPU only — squarified subdivision is sequential/recursive (Bruls/Huizing/van Wijk). Streaming uses **resquarify** (positions-stable resize) on a throttled cadence, NOT per frame. This is exactly the 258-line `apps/showcase/views/treemap/records.gen.mjs` precompute pulled in-engine.

### (d) Escape hatch (STFT spectrogram, custom WGSL compute)

```json
{
  "dataset": { "id": "audio", "columns": [{ "name": "sample", "dtype": "f32" }] },
  "transforms": [
    { "id": "stft", "transform": "customCompute",
      "wgsl": "@group(0) @binding(0) var<storage,read> samp: array<f32>;\n@group(0) @binding(1) var<storage,read_write> mag: array<f32>;\n@group(0) @binding(2) var<uniform> P: Params;\n@compute @workgroup_size(256)\nfn main(@builtin(global_invocation_id) gid: vec3<u32>) {\n  let frame = gid.x; let k = gid.y;\n  if (k >= P.bins) { return; }\n  var re = 0.0; var im = 0.0;\n  for (var n: u32 = 0u; n < P.fft; n = n + 1u) {\n    let w = 0.5 - 0.5 * cos(6.2831853 * f32(n) / f32(P.fft - 1u));\n    let x = samp[frame * P.hop + n] * w;\n    let ang = -6.2831853 * f32(k) * f32(n) / f32(P.fft);\n    re = re + x * cos(ang); im = im + x * sin(ang);\n  }\n  mag[frame * P.bins + k] = sqrt(re*re + im*im);\n}",
      "inputs":  [{ "binding": 0, "column": "audio.sample", "dtype": "f32", "access": "read" }],
      "outputs": [{ "binding": 1, "name": "mag", "dtype": "f32",
                    "length": "frames * bins", "access": "write" }],
      "uniforms": { "binding": 2, "fft": 1024, "hop": 256, "bins": 513 },
      "dispatch": { "x": "frames", "y": "bins", "z": 1 },
      "recomputePolicy": { "onHop": 256 }
    }
  ],
  "encode": {
    "mark": "rect", "pipeline": "instancedRect@1",
    "channels": {
      "x":   { "scale": { "type": "linear", "domain": "auto" }, "field": "frameIndex" },
      "y":   { "scale": { "type": "linear", "domain": "auto" }, "field": "binIndex" },
      "fill": { "scale": { "type": "sequential", "scheme": "magma", "domain": "auto" }, "field": "stft.mag" }
    }
  }
}
```
Notes: a naive O(F²) DFT is shown for clarity; production uses Stockham radix-2 FFT (O(F log F)/hop). Validation enforces `workgroup_size=256 ≤ 256` and storage-buffer-only I/O. Output `mag` lands either in per-instance fill (needs the **per-instance-color rect-grid mark** that VIEWS-DECOMP flags as the cheapest high-leverage gap) or, today, in a `texturedQuad@1` raster. This is the 279-line `apps/showcase/views/spectrogram` precompute pulled in-engine — the one transform no declarative grammar provides natively (PRIOR-ART confirmed gap).

## 6. Key design decisions / honest limits

- **The mark layer needs one new primitive to unlock the field tier cheaply:** a per-instance-color (and per-instance-offset) instanced-rect format. VIEWS-DECOMP shows weather-radar/correlation/footprint/spectrogram-color collapse to native once a per-instance color channel exists — that is a *mark/encoding* gap, distinct from the *compute* escape hatch. Without it, field outputs still route through `texturedQuad`.
- **Layout transforms are not GPU-parallelizable** (except force) and stay CPU; their cost is amortized by throttled recompute, not per-frame. This means treemap/sankey under a fast stream show stepped (not continuous) relayout — acceptable and matches d3 resquarify behavior.
- **Variable-cardinality outputs** (contour, voronoi, custom kernels) need engine-owned max-capacity buffers + atomic counters + a compaction pass; the manifest declares the cap. This is the one place the pure functional column model leaks (output length isn't statically known) and the escape hatch must own buffer sizing.
- **f32-only on GPU** forces time/epoch math onto the CPU side; any GPU transform consuming time must take a pre-normalized relative-f32 column.
- **Universality verdict:** the core+layout+field tiers cover ~80–90% of the corpus as composable manifests; the spectral tail and bespoke layouts genuinely require the custom-WGSL escape hatch. So the answer to the pivotal question is *yes, with a mandatory escape hatch* — consistent with every prior-art grammar.
keyFacts: Insertion seam: transform pass goes between syncAllBuffers() and renderer_->render() at core/wasm/dc_engine_host.cpp lines 218-220; existing CPU recipes (core/src/data/CandleAggregator.cpp, AggregationManager.cpp, DerivedBuffer.cpp) already do hardcoded versions of this and are the negative-space precedent.

Terminal encode nodes must pack to one of 8 fixed vertex formats (core/include/dc/scene/Geometry.hpp: Pos2_Clip 8B, Rect4 16B, Candle6 24B, Glyph8 32B, Pos2Alpha 12B, Pos2Color4 24B, Pos2Uv4 16B); CommandProcessor::validateDrawItem (CommandProcessor.cpp:570-576) hard-rejects format mismatches, so the encoder owns final byte-packing, not the transform.

Tier-to-execution mapping: CORE = WASM-CPU v1 with GPU-compute fast path for large-N (bin via atomic-shared-bins, aggregate via parallel reduction, stack via decoupled-lookback prefix-sum); LAYOUT = WASM-CPU (sequential/recursive, no parallelism) except force = GPU N-body stateful; SPATIAL/FIELD (kde/contour/hexbin/regrid) = prime GPU-compute target; SPECTRAL (fft/stft) = canonical custom escape hatch.

Adding GPU compute is additive not a platform gap: DawnDevice already holds live wgpu::Device/Queue (DawnDevice.hpp:246) and waitUntil() async readback pump exists; the only prerequisite is extending buffer usage to include BufferUsage::Storage (today all buffers are Vertex|Index|CopyDst|CopySrc, DawnDevice.cpp:25) plus a createComputePipeline/beginComputePass path on GpuDevice.

Dataflow = typed column DAG: Column={name,dtype,length,bytes}, dtype in {f32,i32,u32,u8,f32x2,utf8-offset}; transforms are pure nodes inputs->outputs referenced by id.field; intermediate columns live in a transform-side ColumnStore, only final encoded geometry hits store_.setCpuData/writeBufferRange.

Streaming scheduler: class-1 (running min/max/sum/count/EMA/bin-increment) every frame; class-2 (rolling window, STFT per-hop, sliding KDE) on hop boundaries; class-3 (sort, treemap, force, global KDE, contour) on throttled/debounced cadence decoupled from data tick; class-4 (percent-of-whole, global normalization) requires explicit baseline policy {fixedEpoch|decaying|window}.

Escape hatch = manifest WGSL compute kernel with declared typed storage-buffer input/output column bindings; validation enforces WebGPU defaults: workgroup_size<=256, workgroup storage<=16KB, dispatch dims<=65535, <=8 storage buffers/stage, storage binding<=128MiB, f32/f16/i32/u32 only (no f64, so epoch-ms must be CPU-pre-normalized to relative f32).

Variable-cardinality outputs (contour/voronoi/custom kernels) break the static-length column model and require engine-owned max-capacity buffers + atomic counter + compaction pass with manifest-declared cap; this is where the pure functional model leaks.

Expression DSL for formula/filter is a strict side-effect-free subset (no assignment/loops/new, like Vega expression language); same AST compiles to either a WASM-CPU loop or generated WGSL body so formula/filter get the GPU fast path for free at large N.

A per-instance-color (and per-instance-offset) instanced-rect mark is a separate cheap high-leverage gap (VIEWS-DECOMP): it collapses weather-radar/correlation/footprint/spectrogram-color to native and is a mark/encoding gap distinct from the compute escape hatch; without it field outputs still route through texturedQuad@1.

Treemap stays WASM-CPU only (squarified is sequential data-dependent recursion); streaming uses resquarify (positions-stable resize) on throttled cadence, pulling the 258-line apps/showcase/views/treemap/records.gen.mjs precompute in-engine; spectrogram custom-compute pulls the 279-line apps/showcase/views/spectrogram precompute in-engine.

Universality verdict: core+layout+field tiers cover ~80-90% of the chart corpus as composable manifests; spectral tail and bespoke layouts genuinely require the custom-WGSL escape hatch, so the answer is yes-with-mandatory-escape-hatch, matching every prior-art grammar (Vega custom transforms, ECharts renderItem, ggplot2 custom Stat).
openQuestions: Does the per-instance-color rect-grid mark (needed to unlock the field tier without texturedQuad) require a new 9th vertex format + pipeline, and is that in scope for the transform-layer work or a separate mark-layer ticket?

For variable-cardinality outputs (contour/voronoi), what is the policy for choosing the max-capacity buffer cap — manifest-static, auto-grown (hits ENC-558 instanced buffer-growth caching bug), or LOD-bounded?

Should the intermediate ColumnStore be GPU-resident (storage buffers shared between compute passes, avoiding readback) or CPU-resident with per-node upload? Fully-GPU-resident DAGs avoid the f64/readback round-trips but complicate the CPU-side layout transforms that must interleave.

What is the recompute-cadence authority — does the AI manifest author set recomputePolicy per node, or does the engine infer streaming class from the transform type and only let the manifest override?

How is custom-WGSL kernel provenance/trust handled if manifests are AI-authored and potentially adversarial — Dawn validates syntax/limits but a valid kernel can still busy-loop within bounds or produce garbage geometry; is there a per-frame compute time budget / watchdog?

Force layout and t-SNE need persistent simulation state across frames, which breaks the stateless manifest model — does the engine expose an explicit stateful-transform category with engine-owned ping-pong buffers, and how does the manifest declare convergence/reset semantics?

dsl.summary: DynaCharting's raw feed is long-format events {t, streamKey, field, value} (confirmed in apps/showcase/data/market/*.json); today every showcase view precomputes finished geometry upstream (e.g. treemap/manifest.ts hand-codes a 188-line squarified layout + clip-space tessellation). I designed a streaming, GPU-native grammar-of-graphics JSON manifest with eight top-level sections — data, transform, scales, coords, marks (with encodings), facet, interaction, layers — that an AI authors and the engine executes from raw numbers, producing the existing dc_gpu vertex/instance buffers per frame instead of receiving them pre-baked. The core typing discipline is a single named-reference namespace (datasets, transform outputs, scales, fields all addressed by string id with declared column dtypes), so channels can only bind scale(field) of a type the scale accepts, and transforms can only consume an upstream output that exists — making the manifest a typed dataflow DAG that is checkable before a single byte streams. The schema deliberately diverges from Vega-Lite in five GPU/streaming-specific ways: explicit streaming class + recompute cadence per transform, explicit per-mark target pipeline/instance-format binding, an autodomain-with-streaming-policy on every scale, a first-class coords block (affine/polar/geo/projection) replacing Vega's implicit coordinate handling, and a typed customCompute escape hatch (declared WGSL + IO bindings) for the walled tail (KDE, FFT, marching-squares, force/treemap layout). I give full end-to-end manifests for candlestick+SMA, treemap, and node-link force graph entirely from raw data, plus an AI-authorability assessment (it is tractable constrained program-synthesis-against-a-grammar, made verifiable by replaying one manifest against many synthetic feeds and asserting invariants).
keyFacts: Raw feed substrate is long-format events {t, streamKey, field, value} — confirmed in apps/showcase/data/market/AAPL.json (e.g. {t:0, streamKey:'AAPL', field:'lastPrice', value:187.61}; also field:'open'/'close'/'volume'/'depth.bid.0.size'). The manifest's data plane must pivot these into named typed columns.

The existing apps/showcase/views/treemap/manifest.ts is 300 lines, ~188 of which are hand-coded squarified-treemap layout (buildSectors/squarify/worst) + clip-space tessellation — the exact precompute the manifest replaces with a declarative bin->aggregate->treemap transform pipeline + per-instance-color rect mark.

Proposed manifest has 8 top-level sections: data (sources/streams with long->wide pivot), transforms (named DAG ops), scales (domain->range with streaming autodomain policy), coords (cartesian/polar/geo/affineFree), marks (with encodings + explicit pipeline binding), facet, layers (composition), interaction (panZoom drives scale domains, not a baked transform).

Typing is enforced as a typed acyclic dataflow DAG over one id namespace: every from/in/scale/lookup reference must resolve; transform output columns are statically inferable (in-columns minus drops plus as/aggregate.as/formula.as); channel binds scale(field) only if dtype is in the scale's accepted set AND the channel set covers the pipeline's required vertex/instance format — mirroring CommandProcessor::validateDrawItem's hard format rejection.

Five GPU/streaming divergences from Vega-Lite: (1) per-transform streaming class+cadence {incremental,windowed,globalRecompute,iterative,illDefined}; (2) explicit per-mark pipeline/format binding with coverage check; (3) per-scale autodomain.stream policy {fixed,extend,decay,window}; (4) first-class coords block replacing the engine's single baked affine mat3; (5) typed customCompute WGSL escape hatch (Tint-validated, declared IO bindings, sandboxed) for the walled tail.

The candlestick+SMA manifest is fully class-1 incremental (pivot O(Δ), window-mean incremental, running-minmax autodomain) — the 60-120Hz happy path; binds instancedCandle@1 (Candle6 24B) + lineAA@1 from a single window transform with as:'sma20'.

The treemap manifest schedules the squarified 'treemap' op as STREAMING class-3 globalRecompute on a 100ms debounce (cadence:{debounceMs:100,trigger:'onUpdate'}), not every data tick; uses tile:'stable' to keep per-tile color slots continuous frame-to-frame, matching the existing manifest.ts no-sort choice.

The node-link force-graph manifest uses a stateful iterative 'force' transform with engine-owned persistent state (state:'persistent'), a relational lookup so edge endpoints resolve from the sim table by node id, foreign-key ref typing (edges.src/dst -> nodes.id), drag-write-back into transform state with reheat, and the customCompute WGSL escape hatch for inner N-body physics.

Named heavyweight transforms (treemap, force, contour, voronoi, geoproject, fft, kde) are NOT derivable from the basic transform algebra (filter/formula/bin/aggregate/stack/sort/window) — they are engine-implemented primitives the manifest invokes; universality holds iff the engine ships them or the author uses customCompute.

Verifiability strategy: a manifest is a pure function feed->frames, so a test harness replays one manifest against a corpus of adversarial synthetic feeds (empty, single-record, all-equal degenerate domain, out-of-order t, NaN, 1e6-record burst) and asserts feed-independent invariants (finite channels, x0<=x1, in-pane positions, count matches pipeline stride, domain contains all bound values) plus differential/golden parity against a reference CPU evaluator — extending DynaCharting's existing Dawn-golden parity tests.

Time math must stay on CPU: WebGPU has no f64 (WGSL scalars bool/i32/u32/f32/f16) and epoch-ms time overflows f32 mantissa (~16.7M), so the manifest marks time columns role:'time' dtype:'f64' and the compiler keeps time-axis math off the GPU compute path (GPU-COMPUTE brief).

Vega-Lite alignment justifies AI tractability: convergent prior art (Vega ~40 transforms, ggplot2 ~45 geoms) all reduces to ~9 marks / ~13 scales that are well-represented in pretraining, so authoring is constrained slot-filling against a strongly-typed total DSL (no general iteration except named transforms / WGSL hatch) with a compiler-in-the-loop repair signal — the regime where DSL program synthesis converges.
openQuestions: Exact channel->required-vertex-format coverage table per pipeline must be derived from PipelineCatalog.cpp + Geometry.hpp (8 formats: Pos2_Clip 8B, Rect4 16B, Candle6 24B, Glyph8 32B, Pos2Alpha 12B, Pos2Color4 24B, Pos2Uv4 16B); the manifest's pipeline-binding validator depends on this mapping being authored exactly — not yet enumerated here.

The long->wide pivot semantics (rowKey grouping, regex field:capture for node.<id>.weight / edge.<src>-<dst>.weight) imply an ingest-time reshaping stage that does not exist in IngestProcessor today; whether this lives in WASM-side JS, the C++ core, or a pre-ingest router is an open architecture decision affecting the data-sovereignty boundary (embassy packs binary records on-prem).

Streaming class-4 (percent-of-whole, global normalization, fixed-domain z-score) has no well-defined streaming semantics without a manifest-declared baseline policy; the exact policy vocabulary (fixed-epoch / decaying / explicit reference window) and how the AI is constrained to always supply one is specified in principle but not fully enumerated in the schema.

For the force/treemap/iterative transforms, engine-owned persistent state crosses the otherwise-stateless manifest model; the lifecycle (when state resets vs persists across manifest hot-reload, how reheat interacts with retention eviction) needs a concrete state-management contract not fully specified.

The customCompute WGSL escape hatch's security/validation surface (Tint validation is assumed available since Dawn links Tint, but sandboxing guarantees, workgroup/storage-limit enforcement at compile time, and how the AI is given enough WebGPU-limit context to not emit over-limit shaders) is described at a policy level only.

Whether polar and geo coords are implemented as closed-form coordinate-transform primitives in the scale/mark resolution stage or require their own compute pass is unresolved; CHART-UNIVERSE flags geo projection as needing both pre-baked boundary polygons (absent from the value feed) and a non-affine projection function, so the geo coords block likely needs a companion static polygon data source not yet designed.

redteam.wall:
RESIDUAL WALL for networks-hierarchy, in order of severity:

(1) STATEFUL ITERATIVE LAYOUT IS NOT A PURE MANIFEST FUNCTION. Force-directed graphs, Sugiyama layered layout, edge-bundling, force-collide packing, and agglomerative clustering all carry state across frames (position[N] = f(position[N-1])) or require iterative solvers with no closed form. The manifest's own correctness model — 'a manifest is a pure function feed->frames' verified by replaying feeds and asserting invariants (MANIFEST DSL section 4) — is FALSE for these: output depends on accumulated simulation state + init seed, not the feed alone. The design escapes by declaring force as a named primitive with state:'persistent' + an engine-owned storage buffer the WGSL escape hatch mutates. That works, but it means the ENGINE owns the state and the layout, not the manifest — the manifest only NAMES the primitive. So 'one-size-fits-all from a fixed primitive set' holds ONLY IF the engine ships every iterative layout as a named primitive (force, sugiyama, edge-bundle, pack, cluster). Anything not shipped drops to a bespoke escape-hatch WGSL solver, at which point the manifest is a shader-delivery vehicle, not a grammar of graphics. This is fundamental, not fixable by a new declarative primitive.

(2) RELATIONAL JOIN / LOOKUP IS ABSENT AND UNCOMPOSABLE. Every edge-bearing chart (node-link, arc diagram, generic tree links, chord) needs to resolve edge endpoints by joining an edge table to a node-position table by key. The proposed transform layer is per-row map/reduce over ONE table lineage (column = f(same-row input columns)); a key-join across two tables is NOT in any tier of the catalog (filter/formula/bin/aggregate/stack/sort/window/sample/layout/spatial/spectral). The manifest sketches encoding.lookup but provides no transform to implement it. This is a CONCRETE MISSING PRIMITIVE (add a relational join/lookup transform) — fixable, but the briefs underweight it: without it, no edge in any graph can be positioned unless the layout primitive pre-emits edge geometry, which kills generic 'positions + edges -> links' composition.

(3) INTERACTION STATE WRITES BACK INTO THE DATAFLOW. Drag-to-pin a node (writes sim.fx/fy + reheat), hover-highlight, focus+context, collapse/expand subtree, brushing, and cross-view linking are all BIDIRECTIONAL: UI events mutate transform inputs/state. A pure forward dataflow manifest cannot express a write-back loop. The manifest's interaction block can DECLARE bindings (panZoom->scale domain, drag->writes:[sim.fx]) but the actual stateful mutation + propagation is an imperative engine subsystem the manifest only references. Cross-view linking (brush in view A filters view B) is entirely outside a single-manifest model. This is fundamentally outside a declarative manifest; it needs an imperative interaction/state layer, not a new primitive.

(4) VARIABLE-CARDINALITY TOPOLOGY OUTPUT LEAKS THE TYPED-COLUMN CONTRACT. Chord ribbon count = nonzero matrix cells; adaptive Bézier/arc subdivision = variable vertices per edge; orthogonal-routed edges = variable bend points; contour/voronoi cells = data-dependent counts. The pure column model requires output length to be a static function of input length; these are not. The design acknowledges this for contour but it pervades the relational domain. Requires engine-owned max-bounded buffers + atomic counters + compaction — owned by named primitives / escape hatch, not by the composable column DAG.

CONCRETELY MISSING TO EVEN RENDER ANY LABELED GRAPH/TREE/SANKEY/CHORD TODAY: the textSDF glyph atlas is wired to nullptr (core/wasm/dc_engine_host.cpp:321-324), so text marks emit nothing. Every chart in this domain is label-bearing; this is a hard prerequisite gap independent of layout.

SUMMARY OF THE BOUNDARY: After adding (a) instancedRectColor [adjacency/icicle/sankey-nodes/treemap], (b) polar coordinate [sunburst/chord/radial-tree], (c) a curve/arc tessellation transform exposing the existing CurveTessellator (core/src/geometry/CurveTessellator.cpp), (d) a relational JOIN transform, and (e) a wired glyph atlas — the model becomes universal for STATELESS relational/hierarchical layout (icicle, dendrogram-with-pre-emitted-edges, adjacency grid, arc, chord, sankey). It is NOT universal for STATEFUL/ITERATIVE layout (force, Sugiyama, edge-bundling, clustering) — those are engine-owned named primitives or bespoke escape-hatch solvers, NOT composed from the basis — and it CANNOT express bidirectional interaction state (drag-pin, expand/collapse, cross-view brushing) within the declarative manifest at all.

RESIDUAL WALL FOR GEO-MAPS (in order of depth):

WALL 1 — THE POLYGON/TOPOLOGY DATA MODEL (deepest, below the transform layer). The proposed TABLE is equal-length flat typed columns (f32/i32/cat/timestamp). Geographic features are RAGGED NESTED structures: a feature is a MultiPolygon = array of polygons, each = an outer ring + hole rings, each ring = a variable-length array of (lon,lat). TopoJSON additionally carries SHARED ARCS (topology: adjacent regions reference the same border arc). The column model has NO list/ragged/nested dtype and NO topology representation. You can CSR-flatten coords+ring-offsets+feature-ids into columns, but no transform or encoding in the manifest declares or consumes that layout — every transform assumes one-row-per-mark-instance with scalar fields. This is the root cause that makes choropleth, contour-fill, cartogram, and LOD-simplification all hard simultaneously. The briefs never confronted this because the showcase has NO geo view at all (confirmed: apps/showcase/views/ has no map/choropleth/geo) — the domain was never even precomputed-upstream, so it is pure negative space.

WALL 2 — PROJECTION AS A NONLINEAR COORDINATE FUNCTION. The baked mat3 (Types.hpp:39-43) is scale+translate only — not even rotation/shear, let alone projection. Projection (Mercator y=ln(tan(π/4+φ/2)), Albers conic, orthographic) is nonlinear in TWO inputs (lon AND lat jointly). The manifest's first-class coords:{geo{projection}} block is the right instinct but underspecified: for POINTS it is a clean per-row scalar map (so hexbin/symbol endpoints compose), but for POLYGONS it must run as a per-VERTEX function BEFORE triangulation and interact with antimeridian/sphere clipping — and the manifest models coords as a render-time mark-resolution concern, not a transform-DAG stage, so it cannot sequence project→clip→triangulate→fill.

WALL 3 — POLYGON-FILL-WITH-HOLES MARK + EARCUT. No engine primitive fills a concave polygon with holes; triSolid takes pre-tessellated triangles with ONE uniform color (Types.hpp:77). Triangulation (earcut) exists only in demos/drawing-tools, never as a data→geometry transform, is sequential (not GPU-parallel), and is not derivable from {filter,bin,aggregate,stack,window,sort}. Needs a NEW named CPU transform + a polygon-fill mark. (Per-feature color is the EASIEST sub-problem: triGradient per-vertex color, writing one RGBA8 to all of a feature's verts, works.)

WALL 4 — ITERATIVE-PDE + GLOBAL-TOPOLOGY (cartograms, contiguous). Gastner-Newman diffusion cartograms are an iterative PDE solved to convergence, advecting a shared-topology polygon mesh under a global density field. This exceeds even the escape hatch: a single stateless WGSL kernel with static output length cannot express iterate-to-steady-state + topology-preserving advection; it needs engine-owned multi-pass simulation state (like force) PLUS the topology mesh the manifest cannot declare. Dorling (circle) cartograms reduce to force-collision and ARE escape-hatch-feasible; contiguous cartograms are NOT.

WALL 5 — INTERACTION / CROSS-VIEW SELECTION STATE (fundamentally outside a declarative feed→frames manifest). Brush-a-region-to-filter-another-view needs a shared reactive selection signal across panes/manifests. The manifest is defined as a pure function feed→frames (per the MANIFEST-DSL brief's own verification framing); selection-linked views are explicitly NOT a pure function of the feed. This is application/session state above the data→pixels layer; no primitive fixes it because it is a different architectural layer.

NET: hexbin and (square-)bin maps compose cleanly today + instancedRectColor (because their raw substrate is POINTS = scalar rows). Symbol/flow maps and basic choropleth become expressible after adding {ragged-polygon dtype, geo-projection coordinate stage, earcut transform, polygon-fill mark, geodesic-tessellation transform, antimeridian clip} — a LARGE but bounded set of NEW primitives, i.e. 'needs new primitives', not 'impossible'. Contour-over-sphere is escape-hatch (marching-squares, known) + the projection-ordering gap. Contiguous cartograms and cross-view-linked interactive maps are the genuine FUNDAMENTAL walls: the former exceeds the single-kernel escape hatch (iterative PDE over shared topology), the latter is outside a declarative feed→frames manifest entirely (session/selection state). The single most underestimated point: the geo RAW DATA is itself geometry-shaped (polygons/topology), which violates the model's foundational assumption that raw data = scalar feeds — so for this domain the 'NOT precomputed geometry' premise partially breaks at the data-model level, before any transform runs.

THE RESIDUAL WALL for 3d-scientific-fields is a missing RENDER ARCHITECTURE, not a missing transform or mark — and it sits BELOW the layer the entire proposed model operates on. Concretely, three load-bearing engine facts cap the domain: (1) every vertex format is 2D (Pos2_*, Geometry.hpp — there is no Pos3, no z field, bounds are 2-component); (2) the ONLY coordinate transform is a 2D affine mat3[9] whose recomputeMat3 writes scale+translate ONLY (Types.hpp:39 — it cannot even rotate, let alone do mat4 perspective); (3) there is NO depth buffer by explicit design — DawnDevice.cpp:610 states 'no depth: the 2D painter's-order draw has no depth test', the only offscreen attachment being Stencil8 for clip masks. Consequence: the proposed WGSL escape hatch is a COMPUTE escape hatch (storage-buffer in, COLUMN out) and can PRODUCE any 3D geometry's numbers — a projected surface mesh, a marching-cubes isosurface, 3D streamline points — but it CANNOT RASTERIZE them with occlusion, because the only raster path it can feed is the 2D, painter's-order, no-z-buffer pipeline set. A 3D surface/isosurface/volume therefore cannot be drawn correctly from JSON no matter how clever the manifest: self-overlapping geometry needs a z-buffer the engine does not have, and interpenetrating geometry cannot be fixed even by per-frame CPU back-to-front sorting (painter's algorithm fails on mutual overlap). Volume rendering is hardest: it additionally needs 3D textures (only 2D R8/RGBA8 exist) and a ray-generation camera (no mat4). So the true-3D third of the domain (surface, volume, isosurface, 3D streamlines) requires NEW ENGINE INFRASTRUCTURE — 3D vertex formats + a mat4 view/projection uniform + a depth-stencil attachment with depthWriteEnabled + (for volume) 3D-texture sampling and a ray-march render pass — none of which is reachable from a JSON manifest over the existing 10 pipelines, and a custom RENDER shader (vs the existing custom COMPUTE shader) is outside the escape hatch as designed. A SECOND, orthogonal residual wall (it also hits the 2D high-dim views) is interaction/selection state: linked brushing — the defining feature of SPLOM and parallel coordinates — requires runtime, cross-frame, user-authored selection predicates routed into transform filters, which breaks the manifest's pure feed->frames contract and is absent from the model (the interaction block covers only panZoom and drag; pick() returns a single drawItemId, not a row-set). A THIRD wall is t-SNE/UMAP: stateful iterative optimizers with a global kNN-graph + spectral init, which the per-frame pure-function transform model can only host as engine-owned NAMED stateful primitives (like force), not as declarative compositions, and which the stateless-per-dispatch escape hatch does not cover. Net: the model is universal only for the 2D-embeddable subset of this domain; the genuinely-3D and the cross-view-linked parts are provably outside it.

The residual wall for the bespoke-novel-interactive domain is NOT in the data->pixels path — the proposed TABLE+SCALE+ENCODING+TRANSFORM basis (with its own proposed per-instance-color/size additions and the WGSL escape hatch) genuinely covers arbitrary STATIC novel encodings, annotations anchored in data space, and faceted small-multiples. The wall is an entire MISSING ORTHOGONAL LAYER the proposal never models: interaction state + cross-view coordination + per-element identity. Concretely, three coupled capabilities are absent and NONE can be supplied by a transform or a customCompute kernel: (1) SIGNALS/SELECTIONS — named MUTABLE runtime state produced by pointer/keyboard events (brush extents, hovered row, current drill root, camera, transition clock) that transforms and encodings can READ as inputs. The proposal's own MANIFEST-DSL defines a manifest as 'a pure function feed->frames' and converges its vocabulary to marks+scales+transforms only, explicitly dropping the Vega 'signals' tier that exists precisely to solve this. Without signals there is no brushing, no crossfilter, no linked views, no drill-down, no hover/tooltip, no coordinated pan/zoom, no data-driven transition. (2) PER-INSTANCE PICK IDENTITY — verified the engine's picking returns only a 24-bit per-DrawItem id (DawnPickBackend.hpp:48-50); an instancedRect/treemap/scatter is ONE DrawItem, so a pointer hit cannot be resolved to a source ROW. Every interaction that must answer 'which datum did the user touch?' (drill-down, tooltip, brush-select-points, click-to-highlight) is physically impossible without a new per-instance pick id path in the renderer. (3) KEYED OBJECT CONSTANCY + TRANSITION CLOCK — smooth enter/update/exit transitions need frame-N-vs-N-1 keyed diffing and a clock signal; the existing AnimationManager (Tween.hpp) is an unwired scalar std::function tween, not data-bound or manifest-addressable, and the manifest emits geometry statelessly per frame with no per-row memory. The escape hatch specifically CANNOT buy any of this: WGSL compute is a stateless column->column function inside the data path; it cannot capture an event, hold mutable cross-frame selection/navigation/camera state, route a pixel hit back to a row, or coordinate two manifests. So the precise residual wall is: the proposed model is a one-directional data->pixels grammar, and the interactive/linked/animated half of this domain requires a SECOND, orthogonal, event->signal->predicate dataflow plus per-instance pick identity plus keyed transition state — a layer that is fundamentally outside a 'pure feed->frames' manifest and outside the compute escape hatch, and that the proposal does not address at all.

roadmap.summary: DynaCharting today is a pure 2D renderer: 10 fixed-format pipelines, a single baked affine mat3, per-DrawItem single-uniform color, zero compute pipelines, and anonymous untyped byte-buffers — verified against the code (PipelineCatalog, Types.hpp:39/77/80-81, DawnDevice.cpp kStreamBufferUsage with no Storage, "no depth" at DawnDevice.cpp:608-614, 24-bit per-DrawItem pick id, validateDrawItem format gate at CommandProcessor.cpp:570). The target grammar-of-graphics model (TABLE + SCALE + ENCODING + TRANSFORM + new marks + escape hatch) is buildable as a strictly additive layer that compiles down to the existing vertex formats; the renderer stays dumb. A pre-existing CPU recipe/data layer (CandleRecipe, LineRecipe, AreaRecipe, indicator recipes, CandleAggregator, DerivedBuffer, AutoScale, Ema) is the de-risking precedent for Phases 1-3 and means the early milestones are wiring + generalization, not greenfield. The plan is feasible and ships value early: a 7-phase roadmap kills the baked transform in Phase 1, adds the keystone instancedRectColor mark in Phase 2, the CPU transform DAG in Phase 3, GPU compute in Phase 4, layouts in Phase 5, the WGSL escape hatch in Phase 6, and the manifest validator/synthesis harness in Phase 7. The honest universality boundary: the model is universal for the stateless composed-and-walled middle (~70-85% of the chart corpus) given a bounded set of new primitives, but four classes sit permanently outside a pure feed->frames manifest — stateful/iterative layout (engine-owned named primitives only), true-3D rasterization (requires new render architecture: Pos3 + mat4 + depth buffer, not reachable from JSON), bidirectional interaction/selection/cross-view state, and per-instance pick identity (the renderer returns only a 24-bit per-DrawItem id). So the answer to the pivotal question is "yes for the data->pixels grammar, with a mandatory compute escape hatch, but NO for the interaction/3D/iterative-layout layers, which need separate orthogonal subsystems the data->visual layer cannot subsume."

## Engineering Roadmap & Gap Analysis: DynaCharting grammar-of-graphics layer

All repo claims below were re-verified against the working tree at `/home/ndrandal/workspace/Github/DynaCharting`.

### Verified ground-truth (the starting line)

- Zero compute anywhere: `grep -rln "CreateComputePipeline|@compute|ComputePass|dispatchWorkgroups" core/` returns nothing.
- All GPU buffers use `kStreamBufferUsage = Vertex|Index|CopyDst|CopySrc` (`core/src/gpu/DawnDevice.cpp:25-27`) — **no `BufferUsage::Storage`**, the hard prerequisite for any compute binding.
- No depth buffer **by design**: `core/src/gpu/DawnDevice.cpp:608-614` — only a `Stencil8` attachment for clip masks; "the 2D painter's-order draw has no depth test."
- Single baked affine: `recomputeMat3` (`core/include/dc/scene/Types.hpp:39`) writes scale+translate only.
- Per-DrawItem single color `color[4]` (`Types.hpp:77`); the **only** per-instance multi-channel proof is the candle (`colorUp[4]`/`colorDown[4]`, `Types.hpp:80-81`).
- Format gate: `CommandProcessor::validateDrawItem` (`core/src/commands/CommandProcessor.cpp:570`) hard-rejects geometry whose `format != spec->requiredVertexFormat`.
- Pick is a 24-bit **per-DrawItem** id (`core/include/dc/gpu/DawnPickBackend.hpp:4-8`) — cannot resolve to a source row.
- Glyph atlas is `nullptr` in the WASM host (`core/wasm/dc_engine_host.cpp:324`) — text marks emit nothing today.
- **De-risking asset (under-weighted in the briefs):** a working CPU geometry-emit layer already exists — `core/src/recipe/{CandleRecipe,LineRecipe,AreaRecipe,AxisRecipe,BollingerRecipe,MacdRecipe,...}.cpp`, `core/src/math/{Ema,Indicators,NiceTicks}.cpp`, `core/src/data/{CandleAggregator,DerivedBuffer,LodManager,TemporalFilter}.cpp`, `core/src/viewport/AutoScale.cpp`. These are hard-coded recipes that already do "raw numbers -> packed vertex format"; the early phases **generalize** them rather than build greenfield.

---

### (2) PHASED ROADMAP

The ordering kills the baked transform first (Phase 1), then unblocks the cheapest highest-leverage mark (Phase 2), then composability (Phase 3), then the parallel tail (Phase 4+).

| Phase | Goal | Concrete work | Touches (paths) | Effort | Proof-demo |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **0. Prereqs** | Unblock text + storage buffers | Wire the glyph atlas in the WASM host; add `BufferUsage::Storage` to a second buffer-creation path (keep stream path unchanged) | `core/wasm/dc_engine_host.cpp:324`, `core/src/gpu/DawnDevice.cpp:25` | S | Any labeled mark renders text; a storage buffer round-trips |
| **1. Foundation: raw->scale->encode->mark (kills baked transform)** | A manifest with a table + linear/time/band scales + point/line/rect/candle marks renders from raw `{t,field,value}` with no upstream geometry | `TableStore` + long->wide pivot; scale engine (linear/log/time/band/point) with streaming running-reducer auto-domain; encoding compiler emitting existing formats; replace baked mat3 with scale-driven positions (mat3 demoted to pan/zoom viewport) | new `core/src/{data/TableStore,scale,encode}/*`; reuse `IngestProcessor.cpp`, `NiceTicks.cpp`; replace `AutoScale` callers in `LiveIngestLoop.cpp`; **validate against** `CommandProcessor.cpp:570` | **L (~5-7 pw)** | Candlestick + line **from raw OHLC events**, no precompute — reproduces the existing candle showcase view through the manifest path |
| **2. Keystone mark: per-instance color/size + polar** | Per-cell color/size rect-grid + per-point color scatter + polar coords | New vertex format + `instancedRectColor@1` (Rect4+RGBA8) and `instancedPointColor@1`; sequential/diverging color scales emitting RGBA8 per row; polar coordinate slot | `core/include/dc/scene/Geometry.hpp`, `core/src/pipelines/PipelineCatalog.cpp`, `Types.hpp` (coord slot); generalizes candle's two-color proof (`Types.hpp:80-81`) | **M (~3-4 pw)** | weather-radar + correlation-heatmap + footprint + pie **collapse to native** (no `texturedQuad` raster); these are VIEWS-DECOMP KIND-1 walls |
| **3. CPU/WASM transform DAG** | Composable filter/formula/bin/aggregate/stack/sort/window/sample + relational join | Typed `ColumnStore` + topo eval + per-node dirty flag from `ChartSession` touched-buffer set; restricted expression DSL (no loops/assignment) compiling to a WASM tight loop; streaming scheduler (class-1 per-frame, class-2 on-hop, class-3 throttled); **join/lookup** transform | new `core/src/transform/*` incl. `Join.*`; reuse `ChartSession.cpp`, `Ema.cpp`, `DerivedBuffer.cpp`, `CpuBufferStore` dirty-range upload | **L (~6-8 pw)** | Histogram, streamgraph (stack+wiggle), SMA/RSI/MACD, volume-profile **all from raw**; a node-link graph with **pre-positioned** nodes + join-resolved edges |
| **4. GPU compute fast path** | Large-N parallel transforms run as compute passes | `GpuDevice` compute API (`createComputePipeline`/`beginComputePass`/dispatch); parallel reduction (autodomain, sum/min/max/count), decoupled-lookback prefix-sum (stack/CDF/compaction), atomic-u32 histogram bin, 2D KDE splat; same expression AST -> WGSL body; reuse `waitUntil()` readback | `core/include/dc/render/GpuDevice.hpp`, `core/src/gpu/DawnDevice.cpp` (compute path, Storage usage) | **L (~6-8 pw)** | density-heatmap (KDE) + 1M-point M4/min-max LOD at 60-120 Hz, GPU-resolved, no precompute |
| **5. Layout primitives (named)** | Squarified treemap / partition / pack / sankey / dendrogram in-engine | CPU layout transforms (sequential/recursive); `stratify`; resquarify for stable streaming; expose `CurveTessellator` as ribbon/arc transform; throttled (class-3) cadence | new `core/src/layout/*`; `core/src/geometry/CurveTessellator.cpp` | **L (~5-7 pw)** | treemap, sunburst, sankey, ridgeline, icicle **from raw** — replaces `apps/showcase/views/{treemap(258),sankey(232)}/records.gen.mjs` |
| **6. Escape hatch (custom WGSL compute)** | FFT/STFT, contour, bespoke field math from JSON-declared WGSL | `customCompute` node: declared WGSL + typed IO storage bindings, Tint-validated at creation (reject == `frameFailed`-style), sandbox limits (≤256 wg / ≤16KB shared / ≤8 storage / f32-only / no f64), max-bounded output + atomic count + compaction for variable cardinality; Stockham FFT, marching-squares -> line2d | builds on Phase 4; Dawn/Tint already linked | **L (~6-8 pw)** | spectrogram (STFT), contour (marching-squares), density — replaces `apps/showcase/views/spectrogram(279)`, `contour(188)` |
| **7. Manifest validator + AI-authorability harness** | Typed manifest is checkable before any data flows; manifests are testable as `feed->frames` | Validator: ref resolution, column-set inference, channel<->scale<->dtype matrix, channel-covers-format check (mirrors `validateDrawItem`), acyclicity, streaming-class coherence; property/golden/adversarial replay harness (finite channels, rect x0<=x1, domain-contains-values, NaN/Inf rejection) | new `core/src/manifest/*`; extend existing Dawn-golden parity tests | **M-L (~4-6 pw)** | An AI-authored manifest passes the validator + survives the adversarial feed corpus and pixel-matches a reference evaluator |

**Total core path (Phases 0-7): ~35-50 person-weeks** for the stateless data->visual grammar + escape hatch.

**Explicitly deferred / separate programs (NOT in the grammar):**
- **Interaction/signals/selection/cross-view + per-instance pick** — an orthogonal event->signal->predicate subsystem + per-instance pick-id renderer path. XL, architecturally distinct from feed->frames.
- **True 3D** (surface/isosurface/volume/3D-streamline rasterization) — needs Pos3 formats + mat4 + a depth-stencil with `depthWriteEnabled` (today Stencil8-only, `DawnDevice.cpp:614`) + 3D textures + ray-march pass. The compute escape hatch can *produce* 3D numbers but **cannot rasterize them with occlusion**. XL, separate render-architecture program.
- **Contiguous (diffusion) cartograms** — iterative PDE over shared topology; exceeds the single-kernel stateless escape hatch.

---

### (3) RISKS & CRITICAL PATH

**Critical path:** Phase 1 (table+scale+encode) is the spine — every later phase depends on it. Within Phase 1 the riskiest item is the **encoding compiler satisfying `validateDrawItem`'s exact-stride contract** (`CommandProcessor.cpp:570`); get the byte-packing wrong and nothing renders. Phase 2's `instancedRectColor` unblocks the most views per unit effort and should not be deferred. Phase 4 (GPU compute) gates Phase 6 (escape hatch) — the storage-buffer + compute-pipeline API is a shared dependency.

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| **f32-only on GPU breaks time axes** (epoch-ms overflows f32 mantissa ~16.7M; no f64 in WGSL) | High | Keep all time/domain math on CPU; GPU transforms consume a pre-normalized relative-f32 time column. Non-negotiable design constraint. |
| **Streaming recompute cost** — current transforms are full O(N) on every append (`AutoScale`, `CandleAggregator`, `AggregationManager` all full-rescan) | High | Phase 1 replaces O(N) autodomain with O(Δ) running reducers; scheduler runs class-3 (sort/layout/global-KDE) on throttled cadence, not per tick; debounce decoupled from data tick. |
| **Variable-cardinality output leaks the typed-column contract** (contour, voronoi, chord ribbons, adaptive curves) | Medium | Engine-owned max-bounded buffers + atomic counter + compaction pass; manifest declares the cap. The one place the pure column model leaks — owned by named primitives / escape hatch, not the DAG. |
| **Known streaming bug ENC-558** (instanced backend caches GPU buffer per geometryId, ignores growth) | Medium | Real fix = backend dirty/version hook; must land before Phase 2 ships per-instance instanced marks at scale. |
| **Encoding-compiler stride mismatch** vs. `requiredVertexFormat` | Medium | Compile-time format-coverage check in the validator (Phase 7 contract, enforced earlier); golden-buffer parity tests. |
| **Single-threaded WASM** (`CMakeLists.txt:1853`) caps CPU transform throughput | Medium | GPU compute (Phase 4) for large-N; CPU fine for class-1 O(Δ) and inherently-sequential layout. |
| **Escape-hatch security/validity** (hallucinated/malicious WGSL) | Medium | Tint validation at pipeline creation (reject node, no partial render); storage-buffer-only IO; static limit enforcement; no host calls. |
| **AI emits malformed manifests** | Low-Med | Typed DAG + compiler-in-the-loop repair (localized errors), few-shot worked manifests, feed-replay test harness as the synthesis grading oracle. |

---

### (4) FEASIBILITY VERDICT & TRUE UNIVERSALITY BOUNDARY

**Feasible — and strictly additive.** Nothing in the plan requires rewriting the renderer; the grammar layer compiles down to the existing 10 pipelines / 8 vertex formats, and the existing CPU recipe/data layer (`recipe/*`, `data/*`, `math/*`) is a working precedent for "raw numbers -> packed geometry" that the early phases generalize rather than invent. Dawn already exposes compute on the live `wgpu::Device`/`Queue` with the `waitUntil()` readback pump in place, so GPU compute is a one-time API addition, not a platform gap. Value ships early: Phase 1 alone kills the baked transform and renders the canonical financial views from raw data; Phase 2 (the cheapest, highest-leverage milestone) collapses ~6 walled views to native by adding one per-instance-color mark.

**The true universality boundary has four tiers:**

1. **Universal (composable from the basis):** ~70% of the chart corpus — anything reducing to TABLE + {linear/log/time/band/sequential/diverging scale} + {filter/formula/bin/aggregate/stack/sort/window} + {point/rect/line/area/rule/arc/text}. Phases 1-3. This is the convergent prior-art grammar (Vega's basic tier) made GPU-native and streaming.

2. **Universal only via named heavyweight primitives (engine ships them; manifest invokes them):** treemap/partition/pack/sankey/dendrogram (stateless recursive layout), join/lookup, curve/arc tessellation, polar coords. Phase 5 + the Phase-3 join. Composable in the sense of "name it and bind it," NOT derivable from the basic transform algebra.

3. **Universal only via the WGSL escape hatch (stateless compute):** FFT/STFT (no declarative grammar provides it — a confirmed universal gap), 2D KDE field, marching-squares iso-extraction, bespoke per-cell field math. Phase 6. The manifest here is partly a typed shader-delivery vehicle, exactly as every prior-art system (Vega custom transforms, ECharts renderItem, ggplot2 custom Stat) — but GPU-targeted and sandboxed.

4. **Permanently OUTSIDE a pure feed->frames manifest (no primitive fixes these — they are different architectural layers):**
   - **Stateful/iterative layout** (force, Sugiyama, edge-bundling, t-SNE/UMAP, contiguous cartograms): position[N] = f(position[N-1]); the manifest's own "pure function of the feed" correctness model is false for these. They can only be **engine-owned named primitives with persistent state**, not compositions.
   - **Bidirectional interaction / signals / selection / cross-view linking** (brush, drill-down, hover, drag-pin, linked views): requires a second orthogonal event->signal->predicate dataflow that writes back into transform inputs — outside feed->frames and outside the compute escape hatch (which is a stateless column->column function).
   - **Per-instance pick identity:** the renderer returns a 24-bit per-DrawItem id; an instanced grid is one DrawItem, so "which datum did the user touch?" is physically unanswerable without a new pick path. Prerequisite for any interactive selection.
   - **True 3D rasterization** (surface/volume/isosurface/3D-streamline): blocked below the manifest layer by 2D-only vertex formats, mat3-not-mat4, and no depth buffer (`DawnDevice.cpp:608-614`). A custom *render* shader (vs. the custom *compute* shader) is outside the escape hatch as designed.

**Bottom line:** The one-size-fits-all engine is real for the **data->pixels grammar** — yes, with a mandatory GPU-compute escape hatch — and it cleanly covers the composed-and-walled middle that the showcase proved only by precomputing upstream. But "any chart or graph" is **not** achievable from the data->visual layer alone: iterative layout, interaction/selection state, per-instance picking, and true 3D are genuinely separate subsystems. The defensible claim is a GPU-native streaming grammar of graphics that is universal for stateless 2D visualization, paired with (a) named primitives for the recursive/spectral tail and (b) explicitly-scoped orthogonal subsystems for interaction and 3D that the manifest references but does not subsume.
keyFacts: Verified zero compute in the engine: grep for CreateComputePipeline/@compute/ComputePass/dispatchWorkgroups over core/ returns nothing; adding compute is additive (Dawn device/queue already live).

Verified all GPU buffers use kStreamBufferUsage = Vertex|Index|CopyDst|CopySrc (core/src/gpu/DawnDevice.cpp:25-27) with NO BufferUsage::Storage — the hard prerequisite for compute, a one-line addition.

Verified no depth buffer by design: core/src/gpu/DawnDevice.cpp:608-614 uses Stencil8 only ('the 2D painter's-order draw has no depth test') — caps the engine at 2D rasterization regardless of manifest.

Verified per-DrawItem single color[4] at Types.hpp:77 and the only per-instance multi-channel proof is the candle's colorUp[4]/colorDown[4] at Types.hpp:80-81 — generalizing this into instancedRectColor is the keystone Phase-2 mark.
Verified validateDrawItem hard-rejects format != requiredVertexFormat at CommandProcessor.cpp:570 — the encoding compiler, not the transform, owns final byte-packing to the exact stride.
Verified pick is a 24-bit per-DrawItem id (core/include/dc/gpu/DawnPickBackend.hpp:4-8) — an instanced grid is one DrawItem, so pointer hits cannot resolve to a source row without a new pick path.
Verified glyph atlas is nullptr in the WASM host (core/wasm/dc_engine_host.cpp:324) — text marks emit nothing today; a Phase-0 prerequisite since every graph/tree/sankey/chord is label-bearing.

Under-weighted de-risking asset: a working CPU geometry-emit layer already exists (core/src/recipe/{Candle,Line,Area,Axis,Bollinger,Macd}Recipe.cpp, core/src/math/{Ema,Indicators,NiceTicks}.cpp, core/src/data/{CandleAggregator,DerivedBuffer,LodManager,AutoScale}) — Phases 1-3 generalize these rather than build greenfield.

Critical path is Phase 1 (TableStore + scale engine + encoding compiler); riskiest sub-item is satisfying validateDrawItem's exact-stride contract; Phase 4 (GPU compute API + Storage usage) gates Phase 6 (escape hatch).
f32-only on GPU (no f64 in WGSL) means epoch-ms time math must stay on CPU and GPU transforms consume a pre-normalized relative-f32 time column — a non-negotiable design constraint.
Total core grammar path (Phases 0-7) is ~35-50 person-weeks; interaction/signals/per-instance-pick and true-3D are separate XL programs outside the data->visual layer.

Universality verdict: tier-1 composable (~70%), tier-2 named primitives (treemap/sankey/join/polar), tier-3 WGSL escape hatch (FFT/KDE/contour), tier-4 permanently outside feed->frames (iterative layout, interaction/selection state, per-instance pick, true-3D rasterization).

Phase 2 (instancedRectColor + instancedPointColor + polar) is the cheapest highest-leverage milestone: collapses weather-radar, correlation-heatmap, footprint, and pie from texturedQuad rasters to native marks.
openQuestions: Exact person-week estimates assume one senior C++/WebGPU engineer familiar with the codebase; multi-engineer parallelization across phases (e.g. Phase 2 marks || Phase 3 transforms) could compress wall-clock but not total effort.

Whether ENC-558 (instanced backend ignores buffer growth) is fully fixed in the current tree or still worked around in the showcase — it must land before Phase 2 ships per-instance instanced marks at streaming scale; not re-verified here.

Whether the per-instance pick-id renderer path (red-team requirement for drill/tooltip/brush) should be folded into Phase 2's instanced-mark work or deferred to the separate interaction program — it is a renderer change, not a manifest change.
The exact resquarify/stable-tile streaming semantics for treemap/sankey under fast feeds (stepped vs. continuous relayout) need a product decision on acceptable visual jitter.
Whether a CPU reference evaluator for the Phase-7 differential/golden harness is worth building, or whether existing Dawn-golden parity tests can be extended to manifest-emitted geometry directly.

For variable-cardinality outputs (contour/voronoi/chord), the policy for choosing and surfacing the max-buffer cap (manifest-declared vs. engine-auto-grow) is unspecified and affects both correctness and OOM risk.
